970:
971:def apply_full_capability_benchmark_slate() -> None:
972:    """Make the full-capability slate AUTHORITATIVE over .env / conservative defaults (FLOOR semantics).
973:
974:    Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1-1: ``setdefault`` was WRONG. ``load_dotenv`` (openrouter_client import)
975:    puts .env values into ``os.environ`` BEFORE this slate runs, so a conservative .env default
976:    (e.g. ``PG_MOST_MAX_EVIDENCE=300``, ``PG_AGENTIC_WEB_PER_ROUND=6``) SURVIVED the setdefault and
977:    silently throttled the benchmark below the slate. Fix: every numeric key is a **FLOOR** —
978:    ``max(existing, slate)`` — so a HIGHER operator/.env value (more capability, e.g.
979:    ``PG_LLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=600`` in .env) is KEPT, but a LOWER default is RAISED to full capability.
980:    No silent downgrade in either direction (operator no-downgrade directive). Feature flags in
981:    ``_BENCHMARK_FORCE_ON_FLAGS`` are FORCED ON (a benchmark feature off is a capability downgrade).
982:    Exact-value flags in ``_BENCHMARK_FORCE_EXACT_FLAGS`` are forced to their slate value.
983:
984:    Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1-2: MUST run BEFORE the sweep / live_retriever / state imports, which cache
985:    ``PG_LIVE_CONTENT_MAX`` / ``PG_LIVE_HTTP_TIMEOUT`` / ``PG_AGENTIC_WEB_PER_ROUND`` as import-time module
986:    constants. ``main()`` applies it before ``load_locked_questions()``; ``run_gate_b_query`` applies it
987:    before the sweep import. ``preflight_full_capability`` then validates the LIVE module constants so a
988:    too-late application is caught fail-closed.
989:    """
990:    for name, value in _FULL_CAPABILITY_BENCHMARK_SLATE.items():
991:        if name in _BENCHMARK_FORCE_ON_FLAGS or name in _BENCHMARK_FORCE_EXACT_FLAGS:
992:            os.environ[name] = value                       # force exact value — no silent benchmark drift
993:            continue
994:        try:
995:            current = float(os.environ.get(name, value))
996:        except (TypeError, ValueError):
997:            current = float(value)
998:        os.environ[name] = str(int(max(current, float(value))))   # FLOOR: raise-to-slate, keep-if-higher
999:    # PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN is an import-time constant in openrouter_client (cached before this slate via
1000:    # the role import chain), so the floor above only fixes the env; sync the live module global too so
1001:    # check_run_budget enforces the floored cap and run_one_query's manifest reads it (Codex iter-1 P1-2).
1002:    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import set_max_cost_per_run
1003:    set_max_cost_per_run(float(os.environ["PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN"]))
1004:
1005:
1006:def preflight_full_capability() -> None:
1007:    """FAIL CLOSED if the effective benchmark config is below full capability or unobservable — so a
1008:    silent throttle (the ~40-URL bug) can NEVER reach a paid run undetected. Raises RuntimeError."""
1009:    for name, floor in _BENCHMARK_PREFLIGHT_FLOORS.items():
1010:        # mirror run_one_query's read of the real PG_SWEEP_* knob (defaults 12/12/40 if absent).
1011:        _defaults = {"PG_SWEEP_FETCH_CAP": "40", "PG_SWEEP_MAX_SERPER": "12", "PG_SWEEP_MAX_S2": "12"}
1012:        try:
1013:            eff = int(os.getenv(name, _defaults.get(name, "0")))
1014:        except ValueError:
1015:            raise RuntimeError(f"benchmark preflight: {name}={os.getenv(name)!r} is not an int")
1016:        if eff < floor:
1017:            raise RuntimeError(
1018:                f"benchmark preflight FAILED: {name}={eff} < full-capability floor {floor} — the run "
1019:                f"would be SILENTLY THROTTLED (the ~40-URL bug). Set {name}>={floor} or fix the slate."
1020:            )
1021:    for flag in _BENCHMARK_PREFLIGHT_REQUIRED_FLAGS:
1022:        if os.getenv(flag, "0").strip() not in ("1", "true", "True"):
1023:            raise RuntimeError(
1024:                f"benchmark preflight FAILED: {flag} is not enabled — the feature is dead-by-config "
1025:                f"or its firing is unobservable (tracker off). Enable it before the run."
1026:            )
1027:    # I-ready-017 FX-03 (#1107) Codex iter-2 P1: the cited-span window must stay BOUNDED. An oversized
1028:    # PG_GATE_B_SPAN_WINDOW_BYTES makes _cited_window_text expand to the whole direct_quote — BUG-02
1029:    # whole-doc evidence WITH the cited-span flag on. The slate force-exacts it to the 400-byte policy;
1030:    # this fails closed if it is ever outside (0, ceiling] (force-exact removed / slate value changed).
1031:    if os.getenv("PG_GATE_B_CITED_SPAN", "0").strip() in ("1", "true", "True"):
1032:        try:
1033:            _win = int(os.getenv("PG_GATE_B_SPAN_WINDOW_BYTES", "400"))
1034:        except ValueError:
1035:            raise RuntimeError(
1036:                "benchmark preflight FAILED: PG_GATE_B_SPAN_WINDOW_BYTES="
1037:                f"{os.getenv('PG_GATE_B_SPAN_WINDOW_BYTES')!r} is not an int."
1038:            )
1039:        if _win <= 0 or _win > _BENCHMARK_SPAN_WINDOW_MAX_BYTES:
1040:            raise RuntimeError(
1041:                f"benchmark preflight FAILED: PG_GATE_B_SPAN_WINDOW_BYTES={_win} is outside "
1042:                f"(0, {_BENCHMARK_SPAN_WINDOW_MAX_BYTES}] — an oversized cited-span window expands to the "
1043:                f"whole record (BUG-02 whole-doc evidence with the cited-span flag on)."
1044:            )
1045:    for flag in _BENCHMARK_PREFLIGHT_REQUIRED_OFF_FLAGS:
1046:        if os.getenv(flag, "1").strip() in ("1", "true", "True"):
1047:            raise RuntimeError(
1048:                f"benchmark preflight FAILED: {flag} is enabled — Gate-B report.md would include the "
1049:                f"un-span-verified Analyst Synthesis layer. Set {flag}=0 for the verified-only benchmark."
1050:            )
1051:    # I-ready-002 (#1071) P0: the binding faithfulness verifier MUST be at "enforce" — otherwise the
1052:    # entailment judge does not bind as a drop gate and/or a judge_error fails OPEN (unverified clinical
1053:    # claims ship as "verified"). Fail closed before any spend.
1054:    for mode_env in _BENCHMARK_PREFLIGHT_ENFORCE_MODES:
1055:        if os.getenv(mode_env, "off").strip().lower() != "enforce":
1056:            raise RuntimeError(
1057:                f"benchmark preflight FAILED: {mode_env}={os.getenv(mode_env)!r} != 'enforce' — the "
1058:                f"BINDING faithfulness verifier is degraded (entailment not binding or judge_error fails "
1059:                f"OPEN). Set {mode_env}=enforce before the run."
1060:            )
1061:    # Codex diff-gate I-cap-005 P1-2: validate the EFFECTIVE (live) per-run budget cap — reads the
1062:    # module global the guard actually enforces (synced by the slate via set_max_cost_per_run), NOT just
1063:    # the env. Catches a stale-$10 cap that would silently abort a full-depth paid run mid-way.
1064:    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import get_max_cost_per_run
1065:    _eff_cap = get_max_cost_per_run()
1066:    if _eff_cap < _BENCHMARK_MIN_COST_CAP_USD:
1067:        raise RuntimeError(
1068:            f"benchmark preflight FAILED: effective PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN=${_eff_cap:.2f} < "
1069:            f"${_BENCHMARK_MIN_COST_CAP_USD:.2f} floor — a full-depth run would abort early on the "
1070:            f"stale default. The slate must call set_max_cost_per_run() before the guard enforces it."
1071:        )
1072:    # Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1-1: extra CALL-TIME env floors that .env was silently winning over the
1073:    # slate (e.g. PG_MOST_MAX_EVIDENCE=300 in .env survived the old setdefault). The floor-slate raised
1074:    # them; validate so a regression cannot re-introduce the throttle.
1075:    for name, floor in _BENCHMARK_EXTRA_ENV_FLOORS.items():
1076:        try:
1077:            eff = int(float(os.getenv(name, "0")))
1078:        except ValueError:
1079:            raise RuntimeError(f"benchmark preflight: {name}={os.getenv(name)!r} is not numeric")
1080:        if eff < floor:
1081:            raise RuntimeError(
1082:                f"benchmark preflight FAILED: {name}={eff} < full-capability floor {floor} — .env was "
1083:                f"silently throttling it. The floor-slate must raise it before the run."
1084:            )
1085:    # I-ready-004 (#1078) Codex brief P1-2: PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR is a FLOAT in (0,1] (NOT an int floor — the
1086:    # numeric-FLOOR slate path would coerce 0.30 -> 0). When capped finding-dedup is on (required above),
1087:    # validate it via the canonical parser so a malformed/out-of-range value ("0", "1.5", "abc") fails
1088:    # CLOSED before any spend rather than crashing mid-run or sending an unbounded pool. (None/empty ->
1089:    # the parser's 0.30 default, which is valid.)
1090:    if os.getenv("PG_CAPPED_FINDING_DEDUP", "0").strip() in ("1", "true", "True"):
1091:        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.evidence_selector import parse_relevance_floor
1092:        try:
1093:            parse_relevance_floor(os.getenv("PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR"))
1094:        except ValueError as _floor_exc:
1095:            raise RuntimeError(

 succeeded in 964ms:
1160:# faithfulness is enforced by the verify gates (strict_verify / 4-role D8 / provenance / two-family),
1161:# NOT by source-type purity. The drb_72 paid re-run proved the harm: the GENERAL corpus was adequate
1162:# ("adequacy=proceed uncovered=0"), but the journal-only distinct-journal COUNT floor (5<12 — itself a
1163:# §-1.1-banned metadata-as-quality proxy) starved it and forced abort_corpus_inadequate.
1164:# The NAMED-constant + apply_journal_only_for_slug() mechanism is KEPT but dormant (LAW VI): a future
1165:# DELIBERATE, operator-approved journal-only question is re-enabled by appending its slug here (and
1166:# giving its domain template a journal_only profile). journal_only_active() still requires BOTH this
1167:# runtime flag AND the protocol field, so with an empty set nothing activates.
1168:JOURNAL_ONLY_BENCHMARK_SLUGS: frozenset[str] = frozenset()
1169:
1170:
1171:def apply_journal_only_for_slug(slug: str) -> bool:
1172:    """DETERMINISTICALLY set/clear the journal_only runtime flag for ONE benchmark slug.
1173:
1174:    Sets ``PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_JOURNAL_ONLY=1`` iff ``slug`` is a journal_only benchmark slug
1175:    (``JOURNAL_ONLY_BENCHMARK_SLUGS``); otherwise it REMOVES the flag from the env so a value
1176:    left over from a prior journal_only slug in the SAME process (e.g. the ``--all`` loop, or a
1177:    conservative ``.env`` default) can NEVER carry into a non-journal slug. Returns True iff the
1178:    flag was set ON for this slug.
1179:
1180:    This is the per-question seam (mirrors how the slate is applied per ``run_gate_b_query``): it
1181:    runs INSIDE ``run_gate_b_query`` so the flag is correct for exactly the question being run,
1182:    NOT added to the always-on global ``_FULL_CAPABILITY_BENCHMARK_SLATE`` (which would blanket-
1183:    activate journal_only for every workforce query and kill the generic T3 path). The downstream
1184:    consumer (``run_honest_sweep_r3.run_one_query`` -> ``_jo_active`` -> ``journal_only_active``)
1185:    reads ``JOURNAL_ONLY_FLAG`` from the env at run time AND requires the protocol's
1186:    ``source_restriction: journal_only``, so setting it here before the ``run_one_query`` call is
1187:    what makes journal_only fire for the journal-only slug only. Default non-journal runs are
1188:    byte-identical (the flag is removed, not set).
1189:    """
1190:    if slug in JOURNAL_ONLY_BENCHMARK_SLUGS:
1191:        os.environ[JOURNAL_ONLY_FLAG] = "1"
1192:        return True
1193:    os.environ.pop(JOURNAL_ONLY_FLAG, None)
1194:    return False

1197:async def run_gate_b_query(
1198:    q: dict,
1199:    out_root: Path,
1200:    *,
1201:    transport: OpenRouterRoleTransport | OpenAICompatibleRoleTransport | None = None,
1202:    d8_config_path: str | Path | None = None,
1203:    query_index: int | None = None,
1204:    query_total: int | None = None,
1205:) -> dict:
1206:    """Run ONE query through the honest sweep with the native 4-role Gate-B seam ACTIVE.
1207:
1208:    WIRING ONLY (LAW VII CLI isolation): activates `PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE`, builds the verifier
1209:    `transport` (unless one is injected — the offline seam test injects a FAKE), builds the
1210:    argument-taking Gate-B builder closure, and hands transport + builder into `run_one_query`.
1211:    The seam calls the builder AFTER generation with the run-local objects.
1212:
1213:    Transport routing is ENV-gated (`PG_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT`, I-meta-007d): "openrouter"
1214:    (DEFAULT, dev/benchmark — saves GPU cost; OpenRouter is a US router, dev/benchmark ONLY) or
1215:    "self_host" (the lock's sovereign route). The LIVE preflight matches the mode
1216:    (`preflight_four_role_transport`).
1217:
1218:    This function is the Gate-B production entrypoint; its LIVE invocation (real endpoints + real
1219:    spend) is the later operator-authorized canary. It is NEVER invoked against a live endpoint by
1220:    any test — the seam test exercises `run_four_role_seam` with a fake transport directly.
1221:    Imported lazily so this module's import never pulls the big sweep file.
1222:    """
1223:    # I-cap-005 (#1068) KEYSTONE: apply the full-capability slate BEFORE importing the sweep, so the
1224:    # sweep's IMPORT-TIME module constants (content cap / timeouts / workers) also see the full values
1225:    # — not just the call-time PG_SWEEP_* knobs. This is what makes a Gate-B run full-depth regardless
1226:    # of the operator's shell env (the prior ~40-URL throttle was a missing/wrong-named slate).
1227:    apply_full_capability_benchmark_slate()
1228:
1229:    from scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3 import run_one_query
1230:
1231:    enable_four_role_mode()
1232:    # I-meta-007: enable the verifiable quantified-trade-off calculator for the
1233:    # benchmark/paid run ONLY here (gate-B entry), never globally — so the Phase-7
1234:    # Regime-C-verified quantified section actually fires on the paid run.
1235:    os.environ["PG_ENABLE_QUANTIFIED_ANALYSIS"] = "1"
1236:    # I-meta-008 (#1030): activate the contract-driven generation path ONLY for the
1237:    # Gate-B benchmark (entry-scoped, never global — mirrors the line above). Without
1238:    # PG_V30_PHASE2_ENABLED the pre-generation block at run_honest_sweep_r3.py:2779
1239:    # (compile_frame -> fetch_compiled_frame -> compose_outline_from_contract) never
1240:    # fires, so run 5 (a) missed every canonical journal the workforce.yaml contract
1241:    # names (Acemoglu/Autor/Frey-Osborne/Brynjolfsson/Eloundou) and (b) fell back to the
1242:    # clinical default section labels (Efficacy/Safety/Dose Response) instead of the
1243:    # contract section_order (Foundational_Theory/Empirical_Displacement/Generative_AI_Evidence).
1244:    # The three working V30 launchers (run_full_scale_v30_phase2.py, run_phase_g_full_scale.py,
1245:    # run_m_live_1_smoke.py) set this PAIR + the OA-fetch enhancers; we mirror them.
1246:    os.environ["PG_V30_PHASE2_ENABLED"] = "1"   # contract outline + canonical-entity grounding
1247:    os.environ["PG_V30_ENABLED"] = "1"          # V30 Phase-1 coverage report + Methods disclosure
1248:    # OA-abstract / full-text yield enhancers the working launchers pair with the V30 path; the
1249:    # canonical journals are paywalled, so Unpaywall (OA PDF) + Trafilatura (extraction) raise the
1250:    # span-verifiable abstract yield. `setdefault` so an explicit operator override still wins (LAW VI).
1251:    os.environ.setdefault("PG_UNPAYWALL_ENABLED", "1")
1252:    os.environ.setdefault("PG_TRAFILATURA_ENABLED", "1")
1253:    # #1034: paywalled-journal OA fetches are non-deterministic + noisy (Sci-Hub HTML / Jina
1254:    # landing-page markdown / intermittent CrossRef abstract). For frame-contract grounding the
1255:    # clean, deterministic abstract (CrossRef/OpenAlex) is the correct source — contract fields
1256:    # are abstract-level claims. Prefer it over the scrape; setdefault keeps the operator override.
1257:    os.environ.setdefault("PG_FRAME_PREFER_ABSTRACT", "1")
1258:    # BB5-C06 (#1178): prefer-abstract is RIGHT for entities whose contract fields are abstract-level, but
1259:    # it was ALSO skipping the OA full text of narrative / source-critical entities whose substantive claims
1260:    # live in the body (economic/policy/mechanism/cohort/regulatory/legal). Broaden the keep-full-text set so
1261:    # those entities keep the OA full-text path even under prefer-abstract — "never skip an OA full text an
1262:    # entity needs". MUST be set before the lazy frame_fetcher import (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:4567, inside the
1263:    # per-query V30 block) freezes `_FULLTEXT_ENTITY_TYPES` from this env — that import fires AFTER this line,
1264:    # so this placement (next to PG_FRAME_PREFER_ABSTRACT, both before the per-query import) is effective.
1265:    # setdefault keeps an explicit operator override (LAW VI).
1266:    os.environ.setdefault("PG_FRAME_FULLTEXT_ENTITY_TYPES", _BENCHMARK_FULLTEXT_ENTITY_TYPES)
1267:    os.environ.setdefault("PG_OPENALEX_FRAME_FALLBACK", "1")
1268:    # I-cap-002 feature 2/4 (#1060): turn on the ADVISORY analytical-depth annotation for the
1269:    # benchmark/paid run ONLY here (gate-B entry), never globally — so manifest['analytical_depth_
1270:    # advisory'] + analytical_depth.json actually emit on the paid run instead of staying silent.
1271:    # The annotation is non-gating + fail-open, so it can NEVER withhold release. setdefault keeps
1272:    # the operator override (LAW VI); mirrors the PG_ENABLE_QUANTIFIED_ANALYSIS / PG_V30_* lines.
1273:    os.environ["PG_DEPTH_ANNOTATION_IN_BENCHMARK"] = "1"   # force-on (Codex iter-2 P1-1: .env=0 must not win)
1274:    # I-cap-002 feature 3/4 (#1060): turn on agentic URL-DISCOVERY for the benchmark/paid run ONLY here.
1275:    # The agentic loop discovers additional URLs that are fetched VERBATIM via the same seed_only
1276:    # chokepoint + strict_verify + 4-role (notebook/summaries never become evidence). Budget-bounded
1277:    # (content reading forced off + conservative envelope) and fail-open. setdefault keeps the operator
1278:    # override (LAW VI).
1279:    os.environ["PG_AGENTIC_SEARCH_IN_BENCHMARK"] = "1"     # force-on (Codex iter-2 P1-1: .env=0 must not win)
1280:    # I-cap-002 feature 4/4 (#1060) + I-cap-003 (#1066): turn on the ADDITIVE NLI entailment annotation
1281:    # for the benchmark. NLI is the second validator path (catches qualitative-negation hallucinations
1282:    # strict_verify's regex misses); ADVISORY only (4-role D8 stays the single gate). The scoring
1283:    # backend is the frontier LLM entailment judge (PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL, default gemma-4-31b via
1284:    # OpenRouter, family-segregated) — NOT flan-t5/minicheck. We do NOT pin PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL here:
1285:    # pathB preflight requires PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL == PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL and the default already
1286:    # satisfies it. setdefault keeps the operator override (LAW VI).
1287:    os.environ["PG_NLI_IN_BENCHMARK"] = "1"                # force-on (Codex iter-2 P1-1: .env=0 must not win)
1288:    # I-ready-016b (#1097): activate the 3 readiness faithfulness layers for the benchmark. Each only ADDS
1289:    # a layer (safety-refusal classifier / NLI semantic-conflict detection / table-cell numeric verify) — a
1290:    # gate is only ever STRENGTHENED, never weakened. Force-on so a conservative .env=0 cannot silently
1291:    # downgrade the run (operator no-downgrade directive); validated fail-closed by preflight below.
1292:    os.environ["PG_USE_SAFETY_REFUSAL"] = "1"              # force-on (Codex iter-2 P1-1: .env=0 must not win)
1293:    os.environ["PG_SWEEP_NLI_CONFLICT"] = "1"              # force-on (Codex iter-2 P1-1: .env=0 must not win)
1294:    os.environ["PG_SWEEP_TABLE_CELL_VERIFY"] = "1"         # force-on (Codex iter-2 P1-1: .env=0 must not win)
1295:    # I-perm-016 (#1209) KEYSTONE: activate the map-reduce evidence distiller for
1296:    # the benchmark/paid run ONLY here (gate-B entry), never globally. The
1297:    # distiller MAP-validates every finding against the SAME production verifier

3103:            def _planner_llm(prompt: str) -> str:
3104:                # Production Writer call. Build + smoke NEVER reach this path
3105:                # (the planner callable is injected/faked there). One Writer
3106:                # call (plus at most one bounded retry inside plan_research).
3107:                #
3108:                # `run_one_query` is async — the sweep event loop is already
3109:                # running here — so the coroutine is driven on a SEPARATE
3110:                # thread with its own loop (thread-safe; never touches the
3111:                # running loop, which `run_until_complete` would crash on).
3112:                #
3113:                # I-meta-005 Phase 1 FIX 3 (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1 #3): the
3114:                # prior bare `ThreadPoolExecutor(...).submit(asyncio.run, ...)`
3115:                # ran with the worker's OWN empty ContextVar state, so the
3116:                # planner Writer call's billed cost accumulated in
3117:                # `_RUN_COST_CTX` only inside the worker snapshot and was LOST
3118:                # to the parent run (`current_run_cost()` / `manifest.cost_usd`
3119:                # under-reported live planner spend — a budget-cap integrity
3120:                # LAW violation). Fix mirrors `auto_induction.llm_inductor`
3121:                # rounds 3-4 (and `scope_classifier_llm._run_async_in_isolated_
3122:                # thread`): capture the parent context with
3123:                # `contextvars.copy_context()` and run the worker inside that
3124:                # snapshot via `parent_ctx.run()` (READ visibility), THEN apply
3125:                # the worker's cost delta back to the parent context via a
3126:                # closure-shared holder (write-back, fires whether or not the
3127:                # call raised — the OpenRouter client bills partial cost before
3128:                # raising on empty-content/retry).
3129:                import asyncio as _asyncio
3130:                import concurrent.futures as _futures
3131:                import contextvars as _contextvars
3132:                from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (
3133:                    _RUN_COST_CTX,
3134:                )
3135:
3136:                _parent_cost_before = _RUN_COST_CTX.get()
3137:                _worker_cost_after_holder: list[float] = [_parent_cost_before]
3138:
3139:                async def _run() -> str:
3140:                    _client = OpenRouterClient(model=PG_GENERATOR_MODEL)
3141:                    try:
3142:                        _resp = await _client.generate(
3143:                            prompt=prompt, max_tokens=2000, temperature=0.2,
3144:                        )
3145:                        return (_resp.content or "").strip()
3146:                    finally:
3147:                        # Capture the worker snapshot's accumulated cost even
3148:                        # on raise (OpenRouter bills partial cost before
3149:                        # raising on empty-content/retry paths).
3150:                        _worker_cost_after_holder[0] = _RUN_COST_CTX.get()
3151:                        if hasattr(_client, "close"):
3152:                            try:
3153:                                await _client.close()
3154:                            except Exception:
3155:                                pass
3156:
3157:                _parent_ctx = _contextvars.copy_context()
3158:
3159:                def _worker() -> str:
3160:                    def _run_under_ctx() -> str:
3161:                        return _asyncio.run(_run())
3162:                    return _parent_ctx.run(_run_under_ctx)
3163:
3164:                try:
3165:                    with _futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) as _pool:
3166:                        return _pool.submit(_worker).result()
3167:                finally:
3168:                    # Apply the worker's cost delta to the parent context so
3169:                    # the planner Writer spend merges into the parent run cost
3170:                    # (whether or not the worker raised).
3171:                    _cost_delta = (
3172:                        _worker_cost_after_holder[0] - _parent_cost_before
3173:                    )
3174:                    if _cost_delta > 0:
3175:                        _RUN_COST_CTX.set(_parent_cost_before + _cost_delta)

3020:        # (#951) keeps the most relevant candidates within the total cap.
3021:        _max_serper = int(os.getenv("PG_SWEEP_MAX_SERPER", "12"))
3022:        _max_s2 = int(os.getenv("PG_SWEEP_MAX_S2", "12"))
3023:        _fetch_cap = int(os.getenv("PG_SWEEP_FETCH_CAP", "40"))

3025:        # I-ready-006 (#1082): query-complexity router (CAP-ONLY — Codex diff-gate iter-5 §-1.2 rule 6).
3026:        # Default OFF (PG_COMPLEXITY_ROUTING) -> the full heavyweight path, BYTE-IDENTICAL. When ON, a
3027:        # confidently-SIMPLE factual query gets a lower FETCH CAP (PG_SIMPLE_FETCH_CAP) only — a cost/
3028:        # latency saving. The corpus-ADEQUACY gate is DELIBERATELY UNCHANGED: a keyword classifier could
3029:        # not reliably exclude clinical cohort queries ("population of asthmatics/smokers/diabetics"), so
3030:        # relaxing adequacy for "simple" risked under-serving a mis-classified clinical query (the §-1.1
3031:        # lethal class). With the adequacy gate intact, a mis-classified clinical query still hits the
3032:        # FULL clinical bar -> aborts corpus_inadequate SAFELY rather than shipping a thin answer; the
3033:        # fetch-cap reduction cannot breach that bar. The adequacy-relaxation half (a right-sized ANSWER
3034:        # for genuinely-simple queries) is deferred to a follow-up needing a robust/non-keyword
3035:        # classifier or a domain gate. FAIL-OPEN: any router error / confidence below
3036:        # PG_COMPLEXITY_MIN_CONFIDENCE / non-simple -> the FULL path. Faithfulness UNCHANGED throughout.
3037:        _complexity_routing_on = (
3038:            os.getenv("PG_COMPLEXITY_ROUTING", "0").strip() in ("1", "true", "True")
3039:        )
3040:        _routing_decision = None
3041:        _simple_routed = False
3042:        if _complexity_routing_on:
3043:            # FAIL-OPEN (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P2-1): the classifier AND the env parses
3044:            # (PG_COMPLEXITY_MIN_CONFIDENCE / PG_SIMPLE_FETCH_CAP) are inside this guard — a malformed
3045:            # env value or a router fault leaves the FULL heavyweight path intact (the run is never
3046:            # aborted as error_unexpected, and a complex/clinical query is never under-served).
3047:            try:
3048:                from src.polaris_graph.nodes.complexity_router import classify_complexity
3049:                _routing_decision = classify_complexity(q["question"])
3050:                _min_conf = float(os.getenv("PG_COMPLEXITY_MIN_CONFIDENCE", "0.80"))
3051:                _simple_routed = (
3052:                    _routing_decision.complexity == "simple"
3053:                    and _routing_decision.confidence >= _min_conf
3054:                )
3055:                if _simple_routed:
3056:                    _fetch_cap = int(os.getenv("PG_SIMPLE_FETCH_CAP", "40"))
3057:                _log(
3058:                    f"[complexity]  {_routing_decision.complexity} "
3059:                    f"conf={_routing_decision.confidence:.2f} simple_routed={_simple_routed} "
3060:                    f"fetch_cap={_fetch_cap} reasons={_routing_decision.reasons}"
3061:                )
3062:            except Exception as _cr_exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — FAIL OPEN: router/env error -> full path.
3063:                _routing_decision = None
3064:                _simple_routed = False
3065:                _fetch_cap = int(os.getenv("PG_SWEEP_FETCH_CAP", "40"))
3066:                _log(
3067:                    f"[complexity]  router/env error -> FULL path (fail-open): "
3068:                    f"{type(_cr_exc).__name__}: {_cr_exc}"
3069:                )

3071:        # I-meta-005 Phase 1 (#985): field-agnostic research planner (shadow
3072:        # build, default OFF). When PG_USE_RESEARCH_PLANNER is on, the planner
3073:        # produces the frame + faceted sub-queries + archetype outline; the
3074:        # plan is SHA-pinned BEFORE retrieval (gap #19 extension) and its
3075:        # sub-queries are the ONLY non-anchor query source — the legacy
3076:        # domain-keyed expanders (M-28/M-35/trial-DOI/hand-authored) are NOT
3077:        # invoked, the domain_backends router is bypassed (domain=None), and
3078:        # R-6 {domain}.yaml completeness expansion is disabled. OFF: every
3079:        # legacy path runs byte-identically.

3081:            os.getenv("PG_USE_RESEARCH_PLANNER", "0").strip()
3082:            in ("1", "true", "True")
3083:        )
3084:        _research_plan = None
3085:        _planner_protocol = None
3086:        # I-meta-005 Phase 4 (#988): the generator-bound plan + partial flag.
3087:        # Initialized here (OFF + ON) so the generator call is NameError-safe on
3088:        # every path; the saturation loop reassigns them on-mode when it prunes.
3089:        _gen_plan = None
3090:        _partial_mode = False
3091:        _finding_dedup_telemetry = None   # Phase 5 (#989); set just before generator
3092:        if _use_research_planner:
3093:            from src.polaris_graph.planning.research_planner import (
3094:                plan_research,
3095:                plan_sha256,
3096:                serialize_plan_canonical,
3097:            )
3098:            from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (
3099:                OpenRouterClient,
3100:                PG_GENERATOR_MODEL,
3101:            )
3102:
3103:            def _planner_llm(prompt: str) -> str:
3104:                # Production Writer call. Build + smoke NEVER reach this path
3105:                # (the planner callable is injected/faked there). One Writer
3106:                # call (plus at most one bounded retry inside plan_research).
3107:                #
3108:                # `run_one_query` is async — the sweep event loop is already
3109:                # running here — so the coroutine is driven on a SEPARATE
3110:                # thread with its own loop (thread-safe; never touches the
3111:                # running loop, which `run_until_complete` would crash on).
3112:                #
3113:                # I-meta-005 Phase 1 FIX 3 (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1 #3): the
3114:                # prior bare `ThreadPoolExecutor(...).submit(asyncio.run, ...)`
3115:                # ran with the worker's OWN empty ContextVar state, so the
3116:                # planner Writer call's billed cost accumulated in
3117:                # `_RUN_COST_CTX` only inside the worker snapshot and was LOST
3118:                # to the parent run (`current_run_cost()` / `manifest.cost_usd`
3119:                # under-reported live planner spend — a budget-cap integrity
3120:                # LAW violation). Fix mirrors `auto_induction.llm_inductor`
3121:                # rounds 3-4 (and `scope_classifier_llm._run_async_in_isolated_
3122:                # thread`): capture the parent context with
3123:                # `contextvars.copy_context()` and run the worker inside that
3124:                # snapshot via `parent_ctx.run()` (READ visibility), THEN apply
3125:                # the worker's cost delta back to the parent context via a
3126:                # closure-shared holder (write-back, fires whether or not the
3127:                # call raised — the OpenRouter client bills partial cost before
3128:                # raising on empty-content/retry).
3129:                import asyncio as _asyncio
3130:                import concurrent.futures as _futures
3131:                import contextvars as _contextvars
3132:                from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (
3133:                    _RUN_COST_CTX,
3134:                )
3135:
3136:                _parent_cost_before = _RUN_COST_CTX.get()
3137:                _worker_cost_after_holder: list[float] = [_parent_cost_before]
3138:
3139:                async def _run() -> str:
3140:                    _client = OpenRouterClient(model=PG_GENERATOR_MODEL)
3141:                    try:
3142:                        _resp = await _client.generate(
3143:                            prompt=prompt, max_tokens=2000, temperature=0.2,
3144:                        )
3145:                        return (_resp.content or "").strip()
3146:                    finally:
3147:                        # Capture the worker snapshot's accumulated cost even
3148:                        # on raise (OpenRouter bills partial cost before
3149:                        # raising on empty-content/retry paths).
3150:                        _worker_cost_after_holder[0] = _RUN_COST_CTX.get()
3151:                        if hasattr(_client, "close"):
3152:                            try:
3153:                                await _client.close()
3154:                            except Exception:
3155:                                pass
3156:
3157:                _parent_ctx = _contextvars.copy_context()
3158:
3159:                def _worker() -> str:
3160:                    def _run_under_ctx() -> str:
3161:                        return _asyncio.run(_run())
3162:                    return _parent_ctx.run(_run_under_ctx)
3163:
3164:                try:
3165:                    with _futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) as _pool:
3166:                        return _pool.submit(_worker).result()
3167:                finally:
3168:                    # Apply the worker's cost delta to the parent context so
3169:                    # the planner Writer spend merges into the parent run cost
3170:                    # (whether or not the worker raised).
3171:                    _cost_delta = (
3172:                        _worker_cost_after_holder[0] - _parent_cost_before
3173:                    )
3174:                    if _cost_delta > 0:
3175:                        _RUN_COST_CTX.set(_parent_cost_before + _cost_delta)
3176:
3177:            _research_plan = plan_research(
3178:                q["question"], planner_llm=_planner_llm,
3179:            )
3180:            # Pre-register + SHA-pin the plan BEFORE retrieval (gap #19).
3181:            _plan_canonical = serialize_plan_canonical(_research_plan)
3182:            _plan_path = run_dir / "research_plan.json"
3183:            _plan_path.write_text(_plan_canonical + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
3184:            _plan_sha = plan_sha256(_research_plan)
3185:            _log(f"[planner]     research_plan pinned sha256={_plan_sha[:12]} "
3186:                 f"sub_queries={len(_research_plan.sub_queries)} "
3187:                 f"outline={len(_research_plan.outline)}")
3188:            # Frame-derived anchor protocol so planner sub-queries validate
3189:            # against the frame's OWN tokens (brief §2.4 validator adapter).
3190:            _planner_protocol = _research_plan.frame.to_anchor_protocol(
3191:                q["question"]
3192:            )

3259:        _decomposed: list[str] = []

3306:            import asyncio as _storm_asyncio

3308:            import src.polaris_graph.agents.storm_interviews as _storm_mod
3309:            from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (
3310:                OpenRouterClient as _StormClient,

3319:            _storm_state = {
3320:                "original_query": q["question"],
3321:                "region": q.get("region", "global"),
3322:                "web_results": [],
3323:                "academic_results": [],
3324:            }
3325:            _storm_out: dict = {}
3326:            # Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1-a: STORM runs its interviews under asyncio.gather, whose child
3327:            # tasks each get their OWN copied context. ContextVars do NOT propagate cost UP from
3328:            # concurrent children, so STORM's _RUN_COST_CTX spend can be captured NEITHER by a parent
3329:            # copy_context read NOR by a post-hoc check_run_budget — it would silently bypass
3330:            # PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN. Robust fix: book a CONSERVATIVE cost ENVELOPE (scaled by the STORM
3331:            # perspective/round config) into the parent run cost and ENFORCE the cap BEFORE STORM runs.
3332:            # STORM then runs in an ISOLATED context (its real spend is discarded from the parent total —
3333:            # the envelope IS the parent's accounting for STORM). The envelope-inclusive isolated context
3334:            # also makes STORM's OWN internal budget checks stop early if actual spend nears the cap.
3335:            # Conservative by design: over-books, never under-enforces (LAW VI). The per-round estimate
3336:            # is env-tunable.
3337:            _storm_perspectives = int(os.getenv("PG_STORM_PERSPECTIVES_COUNT", "8"))
3338:            _storm_rounds = int(os.getenv("PG_STORM_ROUNDS_PER_PERSPECTIVE", "4"))
3339:            _storm_per_round_usd = float(os.getenv("PG_STORM_PER_ROUND_COST_USD", "0.10"))
3340:            _storm_cost_envelope = max(
3341:                0.0, _storm_perspectives * _storm_rounds * _storm_per_round_usd
3342:            )
3343:            if _storm_cost_envelope > 0:
3344:                _RUN_COST_CTX.set(current_run_cost() + _storm_cost_envelope)
3345:            # Raises BudgetExceededError (-> the sweep's abort_budget_exceeded handler) if the STORM
3346:            # envelope would breach the cap; STORM does NOT run in that case (the raise precedes the try).
3347:            _storm_check_budget(0)
3348:            # Codex diff-gate iter-3 P2: create the client AFTER the budget precheck so a clean
3349:            # envelope-breach abort does not leave an unclosed OpenRouterClient.
3350:            _storm_client = _StormClient(model=_STORM_MODEL)
3351:            _storm_ctx = _storm_cv.copy_context()
3352:            _storm_prev = _storm_mod.PG_STORM_ENABLED
3353:            _storm_mod.PG_STORM_ENABLED = True
3354:            try:
3355:                _storm_out = await _storm_asyncio.create_task(
3356:                    _storm_mod.run_storm_interviews(_storm_client, _storm_state),
3357:                    context=_storm_ctx,
3358:                )
3359:            except Exception as _storm_exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — STORM faults (incl. its own internal
3360:                # BudgetExceededError from the isolated context) never abort the run: the parent budget
3361:                # was already booked + enforced via the envelope above.
3362:                _storm_telemetry["firing_status"] = "error"
3363:                _storm_telemetry["error"] = str(_storm_exc)[:200]
3364:                _log(

3366:                    f"proceeding without STORM queries"
3367:                )

3373:                    pass

3375:                _storm_out.get("storm_conversations", []),

3377:            )

3854:                    # assignments only after every recompute succeeds — so a recompute error leaves the
3855:                    # post-R6 corpus untouched (the outer except is fail-open). Dedup by URL with the
3856:                    # seen-set updated as sources are accepted; only deep evidence rows whose source URL
3857:                    # was an ACCEPTED non-duplicate source are appended (no evidence_row_count inflation).
3858:                    _staged_sources = list(retrieval.classified_sources)
3859:                    _seen_urls = {s.url for s in _staged_sources}
3860:                    _accepted_src_urls: set[str] = set()
3861:                    for src in deep_retrieval.classified_sources:
3862:                        if src.url and src.url not in _seen_urls:
3863:                            _seen_urls.add(src.url)
3864:                            _staged_sources.append(src)
3865:                            _accepted_src_urls.add(src.url)
3866:                    _staged_rows = list(retrieval.evidence_rows)
3867:                    _base = len(_staged_rows)
3868:                    _accepted = 0
3869:                    for ev in deep_retrieval.evidence_rows:
3870:                        _ev_url = (ev.get("source_url") or ev.get("url") or "").strip()
3871:                        if not _ev_url or _ev_url not in _accepted_src_urls:
3872:                            continue  # duplicate / not an accepted source — skip (no inflation)
3873:                        ev["evidence_id"] = f"ev_{_base + _accepted:03d}"
3874:                        _staged_rows.append(ev)
3875:                        _accepted += 1
3876:                    _staged_dist = compute_tier_distribution(_staged_sources, protocol)
3877:                    # I-meta-005 Phase 1 FIX 1 (Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1 #1):
3878:                    # the deepener staged re-check ALSO loads the domain
3879:                    # `{domain}.yaml` checklist via `check_completeness`, then
3880:                    # reassigns `completeness = _staged_completeness` below.
3881:                    # On-mode that would OVERWRITE the neutral report with a
3882:                    # domain-keyed one and re-introduce the banned checklist
3883:                    # label -> generation leak (the deepener can fire on-mode on
3884:                    # a BORDERLINE adequacy decision even with
3885:                    # total_uncovered==0, since adequacy is not gated on-mode).
3886:                    # Gate the re-check: on-mode keep the neutral report (the
3887:                    # merge stays atomic). OFF: re-check runs byte-identically.
3888:                    if not _use_research_planner:
3889:                        _staged_completeness = check_completeness(
3890:                            domain=q["domain"],
3891:                            research_question=q["question"],
3892:                            evidence_rows=_staged_rows,
3893:                        )
3894:                    else:
3895:                        _staged_completeness = completeness
3896:                    _staged_adequacy = assess_corpus_adequacy(
3897:                        tier_counts=_staged_dist.tier_counts,
3898:                        evidence_row_count=len(_staged_rows),
3899:                        domain=q["domain"],
3900:                        protocol=protocol,
3901:                    )
3902:                    # Commit atomically (all recomputes succeeded).
3903:                    retrieval.classified_sources = _staged_sources
3904:                    retrieval.evidence_rows = _staged_rows
3905:                    dist = _staged_dist
3906:                    completeness = _staged_completeness
3907:                    adequacy = _staged_adequacy
3908:                    _log(f"[deepener]    merged +{_accepted} evidence rows (post-chokepoint); "
3909:                         f"adequacy={adequacy.decision} uncovered={completeness.total_uncovered}")
3910:            except Exception as exc:
3911:                _log(f"[deepener]    FAILED (fail-open): {exc}")
3912:
3913:        # I-cap-002 feature 3/4 (#1060): agentic search as URL-DISCOVERY. Default OFF (it SPENDS +
3914:        # makes network calls). The agentic loop DISCOVERS additional high-quality URLs; those URLs are
3915:        # fetched VERBATIM through the SAME run_live_retrieval(seed_urls=…, seed_only=True) chokepoint
3916:        # the deepener uses, so each discovered source earns its tier only from fetched content and is
3917:        # strict_verify'd + 4-role-checked identically. The agentic notebook/summaries are NEVER read
3918:        # (faithfulness: no model paraphrase can become a direct_quote). Budget: content reading is
3919:        # forced OFF (the only un-enveloped LLM term) and a CONSERVATIVE envelope (rounds × per-round)
3920:        # is booked + the cap ENFORCED BEFORE the loop (STORM pattern); the loop runs in an ISOLATED
3921:        # context so its real spend is discarded (the envelope IS the parent's accounting). Fail-open:
3922:        # any agentic/fetch/merge error leaves the post-deepener corpus untouched.
3923:        if os.environ.get("PG_AGENTIC_SEARCH_IN_BENCHMARK", "0").strip() in (
3924:            "1", "true", "True",
3925:        ):
3926:            _agentic_telemetry["enabled"] = True
3927:            _agentic_telemetry["firing_status"] = "attempted_empty"
3928:            import asyncio as _ag_asyncio
3929:            import contextvars as _ag_cv
3930:            import src.polaris_graph.agents.searcher as _ag_mod
3931:            from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (
3932:                OpenRouterClient as _AgClient,
3933:                PG_GENERATOR_MODEL as _AG_MODEL,
3934:                _RUN_COST_CTX,
3935:                check_run_budget as _ag_check_budget,
3936:            )
3937:            from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.agentic_url_harvester import (
3938:                harvest_agentic_urls,
3939:                merge_seed_url_evidence,
3940:            )
3941:
3942:            # Conservative cost envelope: with content reading OFF the only LLM work is the per-round
3943:            # analysis (<= PG_AGENTIC_MAX_ROUNDS calls). Book it + enforce the cap BEFORE the loop.
3944:            _ag_max_rounds = int(os.getenv("PG_AGENTIC_MAX_ROUNDS", "12"))
3945:            _ag_per_round_usd = float(os.getenv("PG_AGENTIC_PER_ROUND_COST_USD", "0.10"))
3946:            _ag_cost_envelope = max(0.0, _ag_max_rounds * _ag_per_round_usd)
3947:            if _ag_cost_envelope > 0:
3948:                _RUN_COST_CTX.set(current_run_cost() + _ag_cost_envelope)
3949:            # Raises BudgetExceededError (-> the sweep's abort_budget_exceeded handler) if the agentic
3950:            # envelope would breach the cap. This precedes the try, so it PROPAGATES (matches STORM) —
3951:            # the fail-open except below must NEVER swallow a budget abort.
3952:            _ag_check_budget(0)
3953:            # PG_AGENTIC_BENCHMARK_URL_CAP is the agentic FETCH lane (budget lane 2/4 of the
3954:            # operator's ~1000-fetch envelope: main 800 + agentic 100 + deepener 60 + R6 40 = 1000).
3955:            # There is NO global fetch counter — each lane caps independently — and in seed_only mode
3956:            # `run_live_retrieval` fetches EVERY seed (seeds bypass the fetch_cap rerank-slice), so the
3957:            # number of seed URLs IS the agentic fetch count. It therefore must NOT be raised above its
3958:            # budgeted value or the ~1000 total overshoots (operator no-overshoot directive).
3959:            _ag_url_cap = max(0, int(os.getenv("PG_AGENTIC_BENCHMARK_URL_CAP", "100")))
3960:            # BB-005 (I-beatboth-fix-000 #1171): the agentic loop discovers ~1933 URLs but only the
3961:            # budgeted _ag_url_cap may be FETCHED. PG_AGENTIC_HARVEST_CAP lets the harvest read MORE
3962:            # of them for DISCOVERY TELEMETRY (urls_discovered_total below) — proving how many high-
3963:            # value URLs the recovery surfaced — while the FETCHED set stays truncated to _ag_url_cap
3964:            # so the fetch budget is untouched. DEFAULT == the fetch cap = byte-identical (no extra
3965:            # harvest, identical fetch). Telemetry-only widening; faithfulness- AND budget-neutral.
3966:            _ag_harvest_cap = max(
3967:                _ag_url_cap,
3968:                int(os.getenv("PG_AGENTIC_HARVEST_CAP", str(_ag_url_cap))),
3969:            )
3970:            _ag_urls: list[str] = []
3971:            # Create the client AFTER the budget precheck so a clean envelope-breach abort does not
3972:            # leave an unclosed client.
3973:            _ag_client = _AgClient(model=_AG_MODEL)
3974:            _ag_ctx = _ag_cv.copy_context()
3975:            _ag_prev_reading = _ag_mod.PG_AGENTIC_CONTENT_READING_ENABLED
3976:            _ag_prev_enabled = _ag_mod.PG_AGENTIC_SEARCH_ENABLED
3977:            # Force content reading OFF (we discard the notebook anyway; this removes the only

3979:            # constants for the call; restore BOTH in finally.

3981:            _ag_mod.PG_AGENTIC_SEARCH_ENABLED = True
3982:            try:
3983:                # Codex diff-gate iter-1 P2: searcher checks PG_AGENTIC_MAX_QUERIES only BEFORE each
3984:                # round, so round 1 would issue every seed if upstream expansion grew. Slice the seeds

3990:                    "region": q.get("region", "global"),
3991:                    "sub_queries": list(_amplified_effective)[:_ag_max_queries],

3997:                    context=_ag_ctx,
3998:                )
3999:                # BB-005 (#1171): harvest URLs ONLY up to the (>=fetch) DISCOVERY cap, then DISCARD
4000:                # the full result immediately so no notebook/summary field is in scope near the merge
4001:                # below (faithfulness defense-in-depth). The discovered set proves recovery breadth;

4016:                _log(
4017:                    f"[agentic]     discovered {len(_ag_discovered)} urls "
4018:                    f"(harvest_cap={_ag_harvest_cap}); fetching {len(_ag_urls)} "
4019:                    f"(fetch_cap={_ag_url_cap})"
4020:                )

4835:        def _compute_m50_skip_anchors(
4836:            contract_plans: list[Any],
4837:            primary_anchors: list[str],
4838:        ) -> set[str]:
4839:            """Codex M-63 REJECT Medium 2: map contract entity ids
4840:            to M-50 trial anchor names via a normalized substring
4841:            match so the sweep can tell M-50 which anchors are
4842:            already owned by a contract slot.
4843:
4844:            Normalization: strip `_`/`-`, lowercase. Match anchor
4845:            against each entity_id; a normalized-anchor substring
4846:            hit inside a normalized-entity-id is strong evidence
4847:            the contract slot renders that trial (e.g.
4848:            `surpass_2_primary` contains `surpass2` → SURPASS-2).
4849:
4850:            Conservative: returns empty set if no contract plans
4851:            present, or if no match found. False negatives cost
4852:            one duplicate subsection (reader confusion, not
4853:            pipeline failure); false positives would SUPPRESS a
4854:            legitimate per-trial section, which is worse.
4855:            """
4856:            if not contract_plans or not primary_anchors:
4857:                return set()
4858:            skip: set[str] = set()
4859:            for plan in contract_plans:
4860:                slots = getattr(plan, "slots", ())
4861:                for slot in slots:
4862:                    for eid in getattr(slot, "entity_ids", ()):
4863:                        norm_eid = (
4864:                            eid.lower().replace("_", "").replace("-", "")
4865:                        )
4866:                        for anchor in primary_anchors:
4867:                            norm_anchor = (
4868:                                anchor.lower()
4869:                                .replace("_", "").replace("-", "")
4870:                            )
4871:                            if norm_anchor and norm_anchor in norm_eid:
4872:                                skip.add(anchor)
4873:                                break
4874:            return skip

4948:        # gate lives in topic_relevance_gate; the production LLM callable mirrors
4949:        # `_planner_llm` (thread-driven coroutine + _RUN_COST_CTX write-back) so the
4950:        # topic-gate spend is NOT lost from the parent run cost. Fail-OPEN: any LLM
4951:        # error / count mismatch / unparseable verdict keeps the batch.
4952:        if topic_gate_enabled() and evidence_for_gen:
4953:            def _topic_llm(prompt: str) -> str:
4954:                import asyncio as _asyncio
4955:                import concurrent.futures as _futures
4956:                import contextvars as _contextvars
4957:                from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (
4958:                    OpenRouterClient,
4959:                    PG_GENERATOR_MODEL,
4960:                    _RUN_COST_CTX,
4961:                )
4962:                # A cheap/fast model classifies title+snippet ON/OFF; falls back
4963:                # to the generator model when no scope model is configured.
4964:                _model = os.getenv(
4965:                    "PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_MODEL", PG_GENERATOR_MODEL
4966:                )
4967:                _parent_cost_before = _RUN_COST_CTX.get()
4968:                _worker_cost_after_holder: list[float] = [_parent_cost_before]
4969:
4970:                async def _run() -> str:
4971:                    _client = OpenRouterClient(model=_model)
4972:                    try:
4973:                        _resp = await _client.generate(
4974:                            prompt=prompt, max_tokens=1200, temperature=0.0,
4975:                        )
4976:                        return (_resp.content or "").strip()
4977:                    finally:
4978:                        _worker_cost_after_holder[0] = _RUN_COST_CTX.get()
4979:                        if hasattr(_client, "close"):
4980:                            try:
4981:                                await _client.close()
4982:                            except Exception:
4983:                                pass
4984:
4985:                _parent_ctx = _contextvars.copy_context()
4986:
4987:                def _worker() -> str:
4988:                    def _run_under_ctx() -> str:
4989:                        return _asyncio.run(_run())
4990:                    return _parent_ctx.run(_run_under_ctx)
4991:
4992:                try:
4993:                    with _futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) as _pool:
4994:                        return _pool.submit(_worker).result()
4995:                finally:
4996:                    _delta = _worker_cost_after_holder[0] - _parent_cost_before
4997:                    if _delta:
4998:                        _RUN_COST_CTX.set(_RUN_COST_CTX.get() + _delta)
4999:
5000:            def _topic_anchor_predicate(row: dict) -> bool:

3058:    from src.polaris_graph.nodes.journal_only_filter import (
3059:        journal_only_flag_enabled as _jo_flag_enabled,
3060:        journal_metadata_entry as _jo_meta_entry,
3061:        canonicalize_url as _jo_canon,
3062:    )
3063:    _journal_only_on = _jo_flag_enabled()
3064:    _journal_sidecar: dict[str, Any] = {} if _journal_only_on else {}
3065:
3066:    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
3067:    # M-INT-1 — Parallel fetch into live_retriever (Phase E1)
3068:    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
3069:    # Wires `parallel_fetch.parallel_fetch(...)` into the content-fetch
3070:    # loop. Per FINAL_PLAN.md M-INT-1: imported, invoked, run-log
3071:    # evidence, and PG_USE_PARALLEL_FETCH=0 disables (rollback).
3072:    #
3073:    # The substrate's ParallelFetcher Protocol expects (bytes, str,
3074:    # int). Live retrieval's existing _fetch_content returns
3075:    # (content, ok, title, body_type, jsonld) — we wrap it in an adapter
3076:    # that stashes the full 5-tuple in a side dict keyed by URL,
3077:    # then post-process serially using the side dict. Diff-gate P1-C: the 5th
3078:    # element is the raw ld+json captured before _strip_html (Signal C input).
3079:    use_parallel = os.environ.get("PG_USE_PARALLEL_FETCH", "1") != "0"
3080:    fetched_side: dict[str, tuple[str, bool, str, str, str]] = {}
3081:    # I-fetch-003 (#1175 / AC3): retrieval-throughput diagnostics. Stay None on
3082:    # the serial fallback / no-candidates path (no parallel_fetch report).
3083:    _fetch_success_rate: float | None = None
3084:    _parallel_completion_rate: float | None = None
3085:    _fetch_workers: int | None = None
3086:    _distinct_hosts: int | None = None
3087:
3088:    if use_parallel and candidates:
3089:        from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.parallel_fetch import (
3090:            FetchOutcome,
3091:            FetchTask,
3092:            parallel_fetch,
3093:        )
3094:
3095:        class _LiveContentParallelFetcher:
3096:            """Adapter wrapping `_fetch_content(url, max_chars)` for
3097:            the parallel_fetch substrate's ParallelFetcher Protocol.
3098:            Stashes the full 5-tuple (content, ok, title, body_type, jsonld)
3099:            in a thread-safe side dict so the post-processing loop
3100:            can read it back per-candidate."""
3101:
3102:            def __init__(self, max_chars: int) -> None:
3103:                self.max_chars = max_chars
3104:                self._lock = threading.Lock()
3105:                self.results = fetched_side
3106:
3107:            def fetch(
3108:                self, task: "FetchTask"
3109:            ) -> tuple[bytes, str, int]:
3110:                # I-meta-007c: surface the per-candidate DOI/PMID hints
3111:                # (stashed in task_metadata at task construction) so the OA
3112:                # resolver can fire on the DEFAULT parallel path, not just the
3113:                # serial fallback.
3114:                _meta = task.task_metadata or {}
3115:                content, ok, title, body_type, jsonld = _fetch_content(
3116:                    task.source_url, self.max_chars,
3117:                    doi_hint=str(_meta.get("doi") or ""),
3118:                    pmid_hint=str(_meta.get("pmid") or ""),
3119:                )
3120:                with self._lock:
3121:                    self.results[task.source_url] = (
3122:                        content, ok, title, body_type, jsonld,
3123:                    )
3124:                payload = (content or "").encode("utf-8", errors="replace")
3125:                return (payload, "text/plain", 200 if ok else 502)
3126:
3127:        # I-fetch-003 (#1175 / BB5-C02): scale max_workers with the candidate
3128:        # count instead of a flat default-8. Explicit env wins; when UNSET,
3129:        # min(_CEILING, max(_FLOOR, len(candidates) // _PER_CANDIDATE)). Named
3130:        # constants (LAW VI — no magic numbers). For ~740 candidates this
3131:        # yields ~46 workers; small corpora stay at the floor of 8.
3132:        _explicit_workers = os.environ.get("PG_LIVE_RETRIEVER_MAX_WORKERS")
3133:        if _explicit_workers is not None:
3134:            try:
3135:                max_workers = max(1, int(_explicit_workers))
3136:            except ValueError:
3137:                max_workers = _FETCH_WORKERS_FLOOR
3138:        else:
3139:            max_workers = min(
3140:                _FETCH_WORKERS_CEILING,
3141:                max(
3142:                    _FETCH_WORKERS_FLOOR,
3143:                    len(candidates) // _FETCH_WORKERS_PER_CANDIDATE,
3144:                ),
3145:            )
3146:        try:
3147:            per_task_timeout = float(os.environ.get(
3148:                "PG_LIVE_RETRIEVER_FETCH_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", "120",
3149:            ))
3150:        except ValueError:
3151:            per_task_timeout = 120.0
3152:
3153:        # I-fetch-003 (#1175 / BB5-C02): per-HOST politeness limit. The
3154:        # parallel_fetch semaphore is keyed by FetchTask.backend_id; keying it
3155:        # by the URL host (below) gives each host its own Semaphore so distinct
3156:        # hosts fetch concurrently while same-host stays capped. Env-overridable;
3157:        # default mirrors parallel_fetch DEFAULT_PER_BACKEND_LIMIT (4).
3158:        _per_host_limit = _env_int(
3159:            "PG_LIVE_RETRIEVER_PER_HOST_CONCURRENT",
3160:            _PARALLEL_FETCH_DEFAULT_PER_BACKEND_LIMIT,
3161:        )
3162:
3163:        fetch_tasks = []
3164:        _per_host_concurrent: dict[str, int] = {}
3165:        for idx, c in enumerate(candidates):
3166:            # I-meta-007c: carry the candidate's DOI/PMID hints into the
3167:            # FetchTask so _LiveContentParallelFetcher.fetch can pass them to
3168:            # _fetch_content for the OA resolver (default = parallel path).
3169:            _doi, _pmid = _candidate_oa_hints(getattr(c, "metadata", None))
3170:            # I-fetch-003 (#1175): key the rate-limit class by URL host so the
3171:            # parallel_fetch host-semaphore yields cross-host parallelism.
3172:            _host = urlsplit(c.url).hostname or "default"
3173:            _per_host_concurrent[_host] = _per_host_limit
3174:            fetch_tasks.append(
3175:                FetchTask(
3176:                    source_url=c.url,
3177:                    backend_id=_host,
3178:                    task_metadata={"index": idx, "doi": _doi, "pmid": _pmid},
3179:                )
3180:            )
3181:        fetcher = _LiveContentParallelFetcher(DEFAULT_CONTENT_MAX_CHARS)
3182:        parallel_report = parallel_fetch(
3183:            fetch_tasks, fetcher,
3184:            max_workers=max_workers,
3185:            per_backend_max_concurrent=_per_host_concurrent,
3186:            per_task_timeout=per_task_timeout,
3187:        )
3188:        # Run-log evidence: persist the substrate's report into
3189:        # api_calls so the manifest sees a non-zero invocation count.
3190:        api_calls["parallel_fetch_success_count"] = (
3191:            parallel_report.success_count
3192:        )
3193:        api_calls["parallel_fetch_errored_count"] = (
3194:            parallel_report.errored_count
3195:        )
3196:        api_calls["parallel_fetch_timeout_count"] = (
3197:            parallel_report.timeout_count
3198:        )
3199:        # BB5-S02 (#1177): surface the leaked-bypass-worker gauge (abandoned
3200:        # in-flight workers that may hold orphan browser subprocesses) into the
3201:        # manifest so the resource-leak signal is auditable, not log-only.
3202:        try:
3203:            from src.tools.access_bypass import bypass_leaked_worker_count
3204:            api_calls["bypass_leaked_worker_count"] = (
3205:                bypass_leaked_worker_count()
3206:            )
3207:        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — telemetry only; never break fetch
3208:            pass
3209:        logger.info(
3210:            "[live_retriever] M-INT-1 parallel_fetch: %d success, "
3211:            "%d errored, %d timeout (max_workers=%d, "
3212:            "per_task_timeout=%.0fs)",
3213:            parallel_report.success_count,
3214:            parallel_report.errored_count,
3215:            parallel_report.timeout_count,
3216:            max_workers, per_task_timeout,
3217:        )
3218:        # I-fetch-003 (#1175 / AC3): NEW retrieval-throughput diagnostics.
3219:        # fetch_success_rate = usable / (usable + failed): a SUCCESS outcome
3220:        # with a 2xx status is "usable"; an ERRORED/TIMEOUT or a non-2xx
3221:        # SUCCESS (the adapter returns 502 when _fetch_content reports not-ok)
3222:        # is "failed". parallel_completion_rate = completed-in-deadline /
3223:        # submitted (1 - timeout_fraction). These are SIBLING fields surfaced on
3224:        # LiveRetrievalResult; they are NOT folded into api_calls (contract
3225:        # stays unwidened, M-INT-1 fields above unchanged).
3226:        _usable_fetched = sum(
3227:            1 for r in parallel_report.results
3228:            if r.outcome is FetchOutcome.SUCCESS
3229:            and (r.fetch_status_code or 0) < 400
3230:        )
3231:        _failed_fetched = len(parallel_report.results) - _usable_fetched
3232:        _denom = _usable_fetched + _failed_fetched
3233:        _fetch_success_rate = (
3234:            _usable_fetched / _denom if _denom > 0 else None
3235:        )
3236:        _submitted = len(parallel_report.results)
3237:        # I-fetch-003 (#1175): a NOT_DISPATCHED task (batch-budget starvation)
3238:        # never ran — it is NOT a completion. Subtract it alongside timeouts so
3239:        # a starved batch reports a LOW completion rate (else the AC3 diagnostic
3240:        # this issue added would paint a starved run as near-fully-complete).
3241:        _parallel_completion_rate = (
3242:            (
3243:                _submitted
3244:                - parallel_report.timeout_count
3245:                - parallel_report.not_dispatched_count
3246:            ) / _submitted
3247:            if _submitted > 0 else None
3248:        )
3249:        _fetch_workers = max_workers
3250:        _distinct_hosts = len(_per_host_concurrent)
3251:        if (
3252:            _fetch_success_rate is not None
3253:            and _fetch_success_rate < _FETCH_SUCCESS_RATE_WARN_FLOOR
3254:        ):
3255:            logger.warning(
3256:                "[live_retriever] I-fetch-003 LOW fetch_success_rate %.2f "
3257:                "(< floor %.2f): %d usable / %d submitted across %d hosts, "
3258:                "%d timeout — corpus may be starved; check per-host limit / "
3259:                "max_workers / per_task_timeout.",
3260:                _fetch_success_rate,

3266:    # #554 (I-bug-115): bound the synchronous post-fetch candidate loop so a
3267:    # wedged per-candidate operation can never hang the run with no terminal
3268:    # verdict. Layer 1 = per-candidate enrich bound (_bounded_openalex_enrich);
3269:    # Layer 2 = this overall wall-clock budget; Layer 3 = per-candidate
3270:    # progress logging below so any future loop stall is diagnosable.
3271:    # I-ready-003 (#1074) P1: the post-fetch loop processes ALL ~fetch_cap candidates serially (each
3272:    # paying an OpenAlex enrich round-trip + tier-classify). A FIXED budget that does NOT scale with
3273:    # fetch_cap silently TRUNCATES the corpus at full cap (the I-cap-005 ~40-URL silent-throttle class,
3274:    # one layer deeper): the slate raised fetch_cap 25x without scaling this wall-clock budget, so the
3275:    # loop hit the deadline mid-corpus, set corpus_truncated, and broke — a "1000-URL" run classified
3276:    # only the first few hundred. Scale the budget with fetch_cap: max(explicit env floor, fetch_cap *
3277:    # per-URL budget). BYTE-IDENTICAL for small caps (the 900s env floor wins when fetch_cap is small);
3278:    # a conservative operator env can no longer silently win because the fetch_cap term floors it.
3279:    _loop_deadline = time.monotonic() + _post_fetch_loop_budget(fetch_cap)

3444:            tier_rule=tier_result.matched_rules[0] if tier_result.matched_rules else "",
3445:            tier_reasons=list(tier_result.reasons),
3446:        ))
3447:
3448:        # I-ready-017 #1134: record the per-source journal-article signals into
3449:        # the journal_only sidecar (ON-path only; keyed by canonical URL). The
3450:        # citeability predicate reads these. OFF → never populated (no-op).
3451:        if _journal_only_on:
3452:            # Codex diff-gate P2: resolve the DOI from candidate metadata, then
3453:            # the OpenAlex work DOI, then a DOI embedded in the URL — so an anchor
3454:            # discovered via a Serper/URL-only path is still credited.
3455:            _jo_doi_resolved = str(_jo_doi or "") or str(oa.get("doi", "") or "")
3456:            if not _jo_doi_resolved:

3458:                if _jo_doi_m:

3460:            _journal_sidecar[_jo_canon(cand.url)] = _jo_meta_entry(
3461:                openalex_pub_type=oa.get("openalex_pub_type", "") or "",
3462:                openalex_source_type=oa.get("openalex_source_type", "") or "",

3464:                is_retracted=bool(oa.get("is_retracted", False)),
3465:                doi=_jo_doi_resolved,
3466:                venue=oa.get("openalex_venue", "") or "",
3467:            )

3469:        # Build direct_quote: head-window (first 1500 chars) PLUS 500-char
3470:        # windows around every decimal in the full content. This way the
3471:        # Phase-4 provenance verifier can find numeric claims that live
3472:        # deep in the fetched HTML (e.g., STEP 5 -15.2% on page 3 of a
3473:        # Nature paper). Without this, strict_verify drops real data
3474:        # because the number it's looking for is outside the head window.
3475:        if content:
3476:            # R-5 Fix D: skip content-starved evidence (PDF metadata,
3477:            # empty body, formatting noise). Passing these to the
3478:            # generator wastes tokens and produces "no extractable
3479:            # text" admissions in the output.

3483:                    "for %r (len=%d)", cand.url, len(content),
3484:                )
3485:                _trace_drop(cand.url, "content_starved")
3486:                drop_reasons["content_starved"] += 1
3487:            else:
3488:                direct_quote = _build_provenance_quote(
3489:                    content, head_chars=1500, window_chars=500,
3490:                )
3491:                _row = {
3492:                    "evidence_id": f"ev_{i:03d}",
3493:                    "source_url": cand.url,
3494:                    "statement": cand.title[:300],
3495:                    "direct_quote": direct_quote,
3496:                    "tier": tier_result.tier.value,
3497:                    "source": cand.source,
3498:                    "full_content_length": len(content),
3499:                    # #956 (S2): the sub-query that surfaced this candidate, so
3500:                    # the evidence selector can reserve per-sub-topic diversity.
3501:                    # Additive only; absent/empty for seed-lane or legacy rows.
3502:                    "query_origin": getattr(cand, "query_origin", "") or "",
3503:                }
3504:                # I-meta-005 Phase 3 (#987): per-row AUTHORITY sidecar. ON-mode
3505:                # ONLY (research_frame present), and INDEPENDENT of the legacy
3506:                # `PG_USE_AUTHORITY_MODEL` tier switch — the plan-sufficiency gate
3507:                # reads the NUMERIC `authority_score`, so planner mode computes it
3508:                # DIRECTLY via the Phase-0a pure function over the SAME `signals`
3509:                # already built for tier classification (no network, spend-free).
3510:                # Honest LOW score/confidence when signals are thin (never a

3512:                # (the legacy domain-keyed gate never reads them).
3513:                if research_frame is not None:
3514:                    _auth = score_source_authority(signals)
3515:                    _row["authority_score"] = float(_auth.authority_score)
3516:                    _row["authority_confidence"] = _auth.authority_confidence.value
3517:                evidence_rows.append(_row)
3518:                _trace_kept(cand.url, cand.source)
3519:

3521:        classified_sources=classified_sources,

codex
Inside `run_live_retrieval`, the fetch itself is parallel, but discovery is not: for every effective query it calls Serper, then S2, then OpenAlex in sequence. After fetch, the OpenAlex enrich/classify loop is also serial, so a 740-URL lane can still spend minutes outside the parallel fetch pool.

255:# Upper bound on a single harvest-loop wait. The deadline is anchored at
259:# a bounded delay of its own start-anchored budget, the harvest loop never
262:# a busy-poll. The actual wait is `min(next_deadline - now, this)`.
267:# Global batch-budget termination guarantee (I-fetch-003 / BB5-C01 P1)
271:# The per-task START-anchored timeout guarantees a STARTED task terminates,
272:# but a never-started task carries a +inf deadline: if every worker thread is
273:# wedged on an abandoned (timed-out but un-cancellable) in-flight fetch, the
274:# queued siblings keep their +inf deadline and the harvest loop could spin
275:# forever. The GLOBAL batch budget is the backstop that guarantees
277:# + queued-siblings. When the budget fires, every still-remaining future is
278:# recorded — started-but-unfinished as TIMEOUT (it ran, over-ran its budget),
282:# Operators override the budget directly with this env var (seconds). When
283:# UNSET, the budget is DERIVED from `per_task_timeout` and the number of
284:# submission waves: `per_task_timeout * (ceil(len(deduped)/max_workers)
286:# needed to run every task once if each task takes a full per_task_timeout; the
287:# slack adds head-room so a healthy batch (tasks finishing well inside budget,
332:# Global batch-budget derivation (I-fetch-003 / BB5-C01 P1)

340:    per_task_timeout: float | None,
342:    """Derive the GLOBAL batch budget (seconds) or None to disable it.
346:      2. Else, if `per_task_timeout` is set: derive from the wave count —
347:         `per_task_timeout * (ceil(num_tasks / max_workers)
350:         per_task_timeout; the slack is head-room so a healthy batch is never
352:      3. Else (no env AND no per-task timeout): None — preserve the existing
353:         "None disables timeout" contract. With no per-task budget there is no
358:    the termination guarantee when a per_task_timeout is in force.
369:    if per_task_timeout is None:
373:    return per_task_timeout * waves

405:    Future.cancel() can't interrupt synchronous code), but
