1:"""Per-claim 4-role orchestration — Mirror -> Sentinel -> Judge, fail-closed composition.
2:
3:I-meta-002 sub-PR-5. This is the COMPOSABLE orchestration layer that drives the three
4:sub-PR-4 role adapters (Mirror, Sentinel, Judge) over ONE injected `RoleTransport` and
5:produces a single `D8ClaimRow` (sub-PR-3) per claim, plus a complete per-call audit trail.
6:
7:There is no direct paid LLM call here; the injected transport performs the (already-paid)
8:call and this module threads its cost into the shared PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN accumulator per
9:I-meta-008 (so generator + 4-role verifier spend are bounded by ONE cap). Tests inject a
10:mock transport. The runtime transport that wraps `openrouter_client` is wired in the sweep
11:surgery (sub-PR-6), NOT here.
12:
13:Two safety properties this module guarantees (both clinical-lethal if absent):
14:
15:1. RECORDING TRANSPORT (Codex iter-2 P1-4). `RecordingTransport` WRAPS the injected transport
16:   and appends one `RoleCallRecord` to `self.records` on EVERY `complete()` — BEFORE returning
17:   to the adapter, so the record exists even when the adapter later raises (Mirror fail-closed).
18:   The Path-B identity gate therefore has no blind spot: every served completion is captured,
19:   including the highest-risk fail-closed paths.
20:
21:2. FAIL-CLOSED FINAL VERDICT (Codex iter-2 P1-1, LOCKED rule). `run_claim_pipeline` computes a
22:   `final_verdict` and writes THAT into `D8ClaimRow.verdict`, while preserving the
23:   `raw_judge_verdict` separately on the result. The rule (documented inline below) makes it
24:   impossible for a hallucination — a Mirror with no grounded citation, or a Sentinel
25:   UNGROUNDED — to reach VERIFIED, while never UPGRADING a worse Judge verdict
26:   (FABRICATED/UNREACHABLE) to merely UNSUPPORTED.
27:
28:`claim_id` is a REQUIRED caller-supplied param (Codex iter-2 P1-2): it is used verbatim for
29:`D8ClaimRow.claim_id` and is NEVER synthesized from claim text (duplicate/edited claims would
30:otherwise collide and break rewrite/gap traceability).

83:def compute_role_call_cost(model_slug: str, usage: dict[str, Any] | None) -> float:
84:    """Cost (USD) for ONE verifier completion from its OpenRouter `usage` block.
85:
86:    Mirrors the GENERATOR extraction (`openrouter_client._finalize_*`, :1717-1751) exactly,
87:    because `RoleResponse.usage` is the verbatim OpenRouter usage block (same shape):
88:
89:    * prompt/completion tokens read directly; **reasoning tokens are NESTED** under
90:      `completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens` when the top-level field is 0 — this is
91:      BLOCKING for Mirror/Judge at xhigh reasoning (reasoning dominates their cost).
92:    * conservative `max(api_cost, imputed)`: OpenRouter omits `usage.cost` for some models, so
93:      the impute path is live; for a budget guard over-counting is the safe direction.
94:    * FLOOR (I-meta-008 P1 / Codex design-review): when the computed cost is 0.0 AND every
95:      extracted token (prompt + completion + reasoning) is zero AND there is no positive API
96:      cost, floor at the ~500/100 shape. The prior `cost == 0.0 and not usage` only caught
97:      None/{}; a NON-EMPTY all-zero usage block (e.g. `{"prompt_tokens": 0, ...}`) would still
98:      record $0 and slip the cap — this token-level guard closes that hole.
99:    """
100:    usage_block = usage or {}
101:    input_tokens = int(usage_block.get("prompt_tokens", 0) or 0)
102:    output_tokens = int(usage_block.get("completion_tokens", 0) or 0)
103:    reasoning_tokens = int(usage_block.get("reasoning_tokens", 0) or 0)
104:    if not reasoning_tokens:
105:        details = usage_block.get("completion_tokens_details") or {}
106:        reasoning_tokens = int(details.get("reasoning_tokens", 0) or 0)
107:
108:    imputed = _orc._impute_cost_from_tokens(
109:        model_slug, input_tokens, output_tokens, reasoning_tokens
110:    )
111:    api_cost = float(usage_block.get("cost", 0) or 0)
112:    cost = max(api_cost, imputed)
113:
114:    # I-meta-008 P1 zero-usage floor: token-level, NOT block-presence. A non-empty all-zero
115:    # usage block (or absent usage) with no positive api cost must be floored, never billed $0.
116:    if (
117:        cost == 0.0
118:        and input_tokens == 0
119:        and output_tokens == 0
120:        and reasoning_tokens == 0
121:        and api_cost <= 0.0
122:    ):
123:        cost = _orc._impute_cost_from_tokens(
124:            model_slug, _FLOOR_PROMPT_TOKENS, _FLOOR_COMPLETION_TOKENS, 0
125:        )
126:    return cost
127:
128:
129:class RecordingTransport:
130:    """Wrap an injected `RoleTransport`; record EVERY completion before returning it.
131:
132:    On each `complete(request)` the wrapper appends a `RoleCallRecord(role, model_slug,
133:    served_model, raw_text, parsed=None)` to `self.records` BEFORE returning the underlying
134:    response (Codex iter-2 P1-4). Because the record is appended before the adapter sees the
135:    response — and therefore before the adapter can parse or raise — the served-identity
136:    record survives even on the fail-closed paths (a Mirror pass-1 that then raises
137:    `MirrorCitationError`). `parsed` is left None here: it is the role contract that parses,
138:    and the parsed sub-results are carried separately on `ClaimPipelineResult`.
139:
140:    I-meta-008 — RUN-BUDGET ACCOUNTING. `complete()` is the SINGLE per-call chokepoint every
141:    verifier completion (Mirror pass-1, pass-2, Sentinel, Judge) flows through exactly once.
142:    After recording, it threads the just-served call's cost into the SHARED `PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN`
143:    accumulator (`_orc._add_run_cost`) and calls `_orc.check_run_budget(0)`, which raises
144:    `BudgetExceededError` BEFORE the next paid verifier call once the generator + verifier total
145:    crosses the cap. The transport itself adds no paid call here — it ACCOUNTS for the
146:    already-served call.
147:    """
148:
149:    def __init__(self, transport: RoleTransport) -> None:
150:        self._transport = transport
151:        self.records: list[RoleCallRecord] = []
152:
153:    def complete(self, request: RoleRequest) -> RoleResponse:
154:        response = self._transport.complete(request)
155:        # Append BEFORE returning so a downstream adapter raise cannot drop the record. The
156:        # served-identity audit trail for the breaching call is preserved even when the budget
157:        # check below raises.
158:        self.records.append(
159:            RoleCallRecord(
160:                role=request.role,
161:                model_slug=request.model_slug,
162:                served_model=response.served_model,
163:                raw_text=response.raw_text,
164:                parsed=None,
165:                # I-meta-002-q1b (#939): carry the separated reasoning so the seam can persist it
166:                # to four_role_role_calls.jsonl apart from the verdict (None if the role had none).
167:                reasoning=response.reasoning,
168:            )
169:        )
170:        # I-meta-008: account THIS verifier call's cost into the SAME run-budget accumulator the
171:        # generator feeds, then enforce the cap. `_RUN_COST_CTX` is per-asyncio-Task; the seam
172:        # runs synchronously inside the same `run_one_query` task that holds the generator spend,
173:        # so the delta lands on the counter holding `generator_spend + verifier_spend`.
174:        _role_cost = compute_role_call_cost(request.model_slug, response.usage)
175:        _orc._add_run_cost(_role_cost)
176:        # FX-11 (I-ready-017 BUG-10b + Codex iter-1 P1): the four-role verifier calls
177:        # (mirror/sentinel/judge) wrote ZERO cost-ledger rows before this. Ledger THIS call through
178:        # the SINGLE canonical writer, which bumps the shared per-session accumulator and appends
179:        # the row atomically — so role-row cumulative is non-monotonic NEITHER across the parallel
180:        # claim workers (each reset their own _RUN_COST_CTX) NOR in file write order. Best-effort
181:        # inside the writer: a ledger failure never breaks the verifier call or the budget check.
182:        _ub = response.usage or {}
183:        _rt = int(_ub.get("reasoning_tokens", 0) or 0) or int(
184:            (_ub.get("completion_tokens_details") or {}).get("reasoning_tokens", 0) or 0
185:        )
186:        _orc.append_cost_ledger_row(
187:            session_id=_orc._CURRENT_RUN_ID_CTX.get() or "no_run_id",
188:            call_type=f"role:{request.role}",
189:            cost_usd=_role_cost,
190:            input_tokens=int(_ub.get("prompt_tokens", 0) or 0),
191:            output_tokens=int(_ub.get("completion_tokens", 0) or 0),
192:            reasoning_tokens=_rt,
193:        )
194:        _orc.check_run_budget(0)  # raises BudgetExceededError if the cap is now exceeded.
195:        return response
196:
197:
198:@dataclass
199:class ClaimPipelineResult:
200:    """The full per-claim orchestration result.
201:
202:    `d8_row` carries the POST-OVERRIDE `final_verdict` in `D8ClaimRow.verdict`.
203:    `raw_judge_verdict` preserves the Judge's pre-override verdict (None if the Judge never
204:    ran because Mirror failed closed). `records` is the complete served-identity audit trail
205:    from the `RecordingTransport` (complete even on fail-closed paths). The raw
206:    `mirror_result` / `sentinel_result` / `judge_result` are preserved for auditability and
207:    are None where that stage failed closed / did not run.
208:    """
209:
210:    d8_row: D8ClaimRow
211:    raw_judge_verdict: Verdict | None
212:    final_verdict: Verdict
213:    records: list[RoleCallRecord]
214:    mirror_result: MirrorPass2 | None
215:    sentinel_result: SentinelResult | None
216:    judge_result: Verdict | None
217:
218:
219:def _first_grounded_citation_id(mirror_records: list[RoleCallRecord]) -> str | None:
220:    """Extract a grounded citation doc_id from the Mirror pass-1 record, else None.
221:
222:    The grounded doc_ids live on the pass-1 `MirrorPass1.citation_spans` (the pass-2
223:    `MirrorPass2` carries only a classification + hash, NOT doc_ids). `run_mirror` returns
224:    its OWN record list whose pass-1 record's `parsed` is a `MirrorPass1`; the first grounded
225:    span's first doc_id is used for D8 gap reporting. Returns None if no grounded span exists.
226:    """
227:    for record in mirror_records:
228:        parsed = record.parsed
229:        if isinstance(parsed, MirrorPass1) and parsed.citation_spans:
230:            for span in parsed.citation_spans:
231:                if span.doc_ids:
232:                    return span.doc_ids[0]
233:    return None
234:
235:
236:def _compose_final_verdict(
237:    *,
238:    mirror_failed_closed: bool,
239:    sentinel_result: SentinelResult | None,
240:    raw_judge_verdict: Verdict | None,
241:) -> Verdict:
242:    """LOCKED fail-closed composition rule (Codex iter-2 P1-1).
243:
244:    A hallucination can NEVER reach VERIFIED; a worse Judge verdict is NEVER upgraded.
245:
246:      (1) Mirror raised MirrorCitationError/MirrorBindingError (no grounded/bound citation)
247:          -> UNSUPPORTED, regardless of Judge. A claim with no grounded citation can never be
248:          VERIFIED. (On this path the Judge never ran, so raw_judge_verdict is None.)
249:      (2) ELSE if Sentinel == UNGROUNDED OR Sentinel.parsed_ok is False
250:          -> if raw Judge verdict is VERIFIED/PARTIAL, OVERRIDE to UNSUPPORTED;
251:             if raw Judge verdict is FABRICATED/UNREACHABLE/UNSUPPORTED, PRESERVE it
252:             (never upgrade a worse verdict to merely UNSUPPORTED).
253:      (3) ELSE -> raw_judge_verdict (Sentinel grounded + parsed_ok; trust the arbiter).
254:    """
255:    # (1) Mirror fail-closed -> UNSUPPORTED.
256:    if mirror_failed_closed:
257:        return _VERDICT_UNSUPPORTED
258:
259:    # raw_judge_verdict is non-None here: the Judge ran (Mirror succeeded). It fails LOUD on a
260:    # non-enum token (JudgeEnumError propagates from run_judge), so it is a valid Verdict.
261:    assert raw_judge_verdict is not None  # invariant: Judge ran iff Mirror succeeded.
262:
263:    # (2) Sentinel UNGROUNDED or unparsed -> downgrade an apparently-good Judge verdict.
264:    sentinel_unsafe = sentinel_result is None or (
265:        sentinel_result.verdict == SentinelVerdict.UNGROUNDED
266:        or not sentinel_result.parsed_ok
267:    )
268:    if sentinel_unsafe:
269:        if raw_judge_verdict in _SENTINEL_OVERRIDE_DOWNGRADE_FROM:
270:            return _VERDICT_UNSUPPORTED
271:        return raw_judge_verdict  # FABRICATED / UNREACHABLE / UNSUPPORTED preserved.
272:
273:    # (3) Sentinel grounded + parsed_ok -> the Judge's verdict stands.
274:    return raw_judge_verdict
275:
276:
277:def run_claim_pipeline(
278:    transport: RoleTransport,
279:    *,
280:    claim_id: str,
281:    claim: str,
282:    evidence_documents: list[EvidenceDocument],
283:    severity: str,
284:    s0_categories: list[str],
285:    model_slugs: dict[str, str],
286:    timestamp: str,
287:) -> ClaimPipelineResult:
288:    """Run Mirror -> Sentinel -> Judge over the injected transport for ONE claim.
289:
290:    `model_slugs` maps role -> pinned slug (keys: "mirror", "sentinel", "judge"). `claim_id`
291:    is REQUIRED and flows verbatim into `D8ClaimRow.claim_id` (never synthesized). `timestamp`
292:    is passed through for the caller's audit record (this function does NOT call
293:    datetime.now()).
294:
295:    Composition is FAIL-CLOSED per `_compose_final_verdict`. On a Mirror fail-closed
296:    (MirrorCitationError/MirrorBindingError) the pipeline SHORT-CIRCUITS: Sentinel and Judge
297:    do not run, `final_verdict = UNSUPPORTED`, and the raw sub-results are None — but the
298:    RecordingTransport still holds the served-identity record for the Mirror call(s) that did
299:    fire, so the identity gate sees them.
300:    """
301:    recording = RecordingTransport(transport)
302:
303:    mirror_slug = model_slugs["mirror"]
304:    sentinel_slug = model_slugs["sentinel"]
305:    judge_slug = model_slugs["judge"]
306:
307:    mirror_result: MirrorPass2 | None = None
308:    sentinel_result: SentinelResult | None = None
309:    judge_result: Verdict | None = None
310:    raw_judge_verdict: Verdict | None = None
311:    citation_id: str | None = None
312:    mirror_failed_closed = False
313:
314:    # --- stage 14: Mirror (fail CLOSED) ----------------------------------------------
315:    # Catch the grounding/binding/parse errors EXPLICITLY (NOT `except: pass`) so they drive the
316:    # UNSUPPORTED override; any OTHER exception propagates (a transport fault is not a verdict).
317:    # MirrorParseError (#1028): a verifier that responded but returned un-classifiable JSON is a
318:    # VERDICT-level failure (fail closed for THIS claim), NOT a transport fault — so one malformed
319:    # pass-2 response degrades a single claim to UNSUPPORTED instead of crashing the whole run.
320:    try:
321:        mirror_result, mirror_records = run_mirror(
322:            recording, claim, evidence_documents, model_slug=mirror_slug
323:        )
324:        citation_id = _first_grounded_citation_id(mirror_records)
325:    except (MirrorCitationError, MirrorBindingError, MirrorParseError):
326:        mirror_failed_closed = True
327:
328:    # --- stage 15 + 16: Sentinel -> Judge (only if Mirror produced a grounded claim) -----
329:    if not mirror_failed_closed:
330:        sentinel_result, _sentinel_records = run_sentinel(
331:            recording, claim, evidence_documents, model_slug=sentinel_slug
332:        )
333:        evidence_text = "\n\n".join(doc.text for doc in evidence_documents)
334:        # Judge fails LOUD by design: a non-enum token raises JudgeEnumError, which we do NOT
335:        # catch — a missing/garbage arbiter verdict must propagate, never coerce to a default.
336:        raw_judge_verdict, _judge_records = run_judge(
337:            recording,
338:            claim,
339:            evidence_text,
340:            mirror_verdict=str(mirror_result.classification),
341:            sentinel_verdict=sentinel_result.verdict.value,
342:            model_slug=judge_slug,
343:        )
344:        judge_result = raw_judge_verdict
345:
346:    final_verdict = _compose_final_verdict(
347:        mirror_failed_closed=mirror_failed_closed,
348:        sentinel_result=sentinel_result,
349:        raw_judge_verdict=raw_judge_verdict,
350:    )
351:
352:    d8_row = D8ClaimRow(
353:        claim_id=claim_id,
354:        severity=severity,
355:        verdict=final_verdict,
356:        citation_id=citation_id,
357:        s0_categories=list(s0_categories),
358:    )
359:
360:    return ClaimPipelineResult(
361:        d8_row=d8_row,
362:        raw_judge_verdict=raw_judge_verdict,
363:        final_verdict=final_verdict,
364:        records=recording.records,
365:        mirror_result=mirror_result,
366:        sentinel_result=sentinel_result,
367:        judge_result=judge_result,
368:    )

 succeeded in 1161ms:
850:        raise BlankVerdictError(
851:            f"OpenRouter response choice carried no/blank message content after reasoning "
852:            f"separation (model={model_repr})"
853:        )
854:    served_model = raw.get("model")
855:    usage = raw.get("usage")
856:    return bare, served_model, usage, reasoning
857:
858:
859:class OpenRouterRoleTransport:
860:    """Sync `RoleTransport` routing the Mirror / Sentinel / Judge verifier roles via OpenRouter
861:    with MAX reasoning (benchmark stage, I-meta-007d).
862:
863:    Implements the SAME `RoleTransport` Protocol as `OpenAICompatibleRoleTransport`. The
864:    `httpx.Client` is INJECTED so tests pass an in-process stub (no network, no spend). Each
865:    `complete()` resolves the OpenRouter endpoint + BENCHMARK-lineup slug (P1-1), normalizes the
866:    payload (reusing the self-host message-rendering, then dropping the top-level `documents` key
867:    per P1-4), POSTs `/chat/completions` with MAX (`xhigh`) reasoning enabled, separates reasoning
868:    from the bare verdict (via `_parse_openrouter_response`, which reads `message.reasoning` per
869:    P1-3), captures the call under the Path-B gate (reasoning sanitized OUT of the capture
870:    channel), and returns a `RoleResponse` with reasoning kept SEPARATE from `raw_text`.
871:
872:    SOVEREIGNTY: OpenRouter is a US router — dev/benchmark ONLY. Self-host (the lock's
873:    `serving_route`) is the final sovereign destination. See the module docstring.
874:    """
875:
876:    def __init__(self, http_client: httpx.Client) -> None:
877:        self._http_client = http_client
878:
879:    def complete(self, request: RoleRequest) -> RoleResponse:
880:        """Perform one OpenRouter completion for `request`. SYNC. Fails loud on error.
881:
882:        Generator requests raise via `openrouter_role_endpoint`. The normalized messages are the
883:        SAME list POSTed to the body AND passed to `capture_llm_call`. The captured raw is
884:        augmented with `_pathb_served={provider, model, system_fingerprint}` so M4's
885:        served==pinned check consumes the genuinely-served OpenRouter identity (not the
886:        request-derived `model`). Reasoning is separated from the bare verdict (I-meta-002-q1b)
887:        and sanitized OUT of the Path-B capture channel.
888:        """
889:        base_url, api_key, model_slug = openrouter_role_endpoint(request.role)
890:        normalized_messages = _normalize_messages(request)
891:        body = _build_openrouter_body(request, model_slug, normalized_messages)
892:        url = f"{base_url}{_CHAT_COMPLETIONS_PATH}"
893:        headers = {
894:            "Authorization": f"Bearer {api_key}",
895:            "Content-Type": "application/json",
896:            "HTTP-Referer": "https://polaris-research.ai",
897:            "X-Title": "polaris graph",
898:        }
899:
900:        # I-meta-008 / #1026: reasoning verifiers get the blank-verdict step-down ladder so a
901:        # non-converging xhigh response cannot crash the whole 4-role run. The non-reasoning
902:        # Sentinel makes a SINGLE attempt (its body has no `reasoning` block to step down) — its
903:        # behavior is unchanged.
904:        reasoning_role = role_reasoning_enabled(request.role, request.model_slug)
905:        # I-run11-008 (#1053): the Mirror uses a FIXED numeric reasoning cap (reasoning.max_tokens), NOT
906:        # effort-stepping — so it must NOT use the effort ladder (which would clobber the cap back to
907:        # effort=xhigh, the no-op that caused the blanks). Only effort-based reasoning roles (the Judge)
908:        # step effort down. The Mirror instead retries to FAIL OVER providers (below), keeping its cap.
909:        uses_effort_ladder = reasoning_role and request.role != "mirror"
910:        # I-run11-007 (#1051): a non-effort-ladder role gets (1 + PG_PROVIDER_BLANK_RETRIES) attempts
911:        # when routed, so a blank from a flaky provider advances to the next HEALTHY provider (OpenRouter
912:        # does not auto-advance on an empty 200). WITHOUT routing it keeps the original single attempt.
913:        provider_blank_retries = max(0, int(os.getenv("PG_PROVIDER_BLANK_RETRIES", "3")))
914:        role_is_routed = role_provider_routing(request.role) is not None
915:        retry_attempts = (1 + provider_blank_retries) if role_is_routed else 1
916:        effort_ladder: tuple[object, ...] = (
917:            _VERIFIER_EFFORT_LADDER
918:            if uses_effort_ladder
919:            else (_REASONING_EFFORT,) * retry_attempts
920:        )
921:        last_blank: BlankVerdictError | None = None
922:
923:        # #1226 (I-pipe-001) — RELIABILITY backstops (kill-switch default-ON; PG_ROLE_BLANK_WATCHDOG=0
924:        # reverts to byte-identical control flow). When ON: (1) TRUNCATE the ladder to a hard ceiling
925:        # of (1 + PG_ROLE_BLANK_MAX_RETRIES) attempts so a custom ladder / high PG_PROVIDER_BLANK_RETRIES
926:        # can never make the blank loop unbounded — at the default cap (4) this is the IDENTITY for every
927:        # role (Judge 3, Mirror/routed-Sentinel 4, classifier 1, decomp 3 are all <= 4). (2) START a
928:        # monotonic-clock wall-clock watchdog over the WHOLE retry loop (checked below at the top of each
929:        # attempt + before each rate-limit sleep). NEITHER affects a verdict: exhaustion re-raises the
930:        # SAME existing fail-loud BlankVerdictError/RoleTransportError -> release HELD; no fake verdict.
931:        _watchdog_on = role_blank_watchdog_enabled()
932:        if _watchdog_on:
933:            _max_attempts = 1 + role_blank_max_retries()
934:            effort_ladder = effort_ladder[:_max_attempts]
935:        _call_timeout_s = role_call_timeout_seconds()
936:        _watchdog_start = time.monotonic()
937:
938:        with _pathb_capture.llm_role(request.role):
939:            for attempt, effort in enumerate(effort_ladder):
940:                # #1226: COOPERATIVE wall-clock watchdog. If the COMPOSED retry loop has already
941:                # exceeded PG_ROLE_CALL_TIMEOUT_S, abort LOUDLY rather than entering yet another (up
942:                # to _TIMEOUT_SECONDS-long) POST and hanging the D8 gate. Re-raise the last blank if
943:                # the loop was blanking (recoverable-shape, fail-closed downstream) else a plain
944:                # RoleTransportError. Never synthesizes a verdict. OFF -> this block never runs.
945:                if _watchdog_on and _watchdog_expired(
946:                    _watchdog_start, _call_timeout_s, time.monotonic()
947:                ):
948:                    raise last_blank or RoleTransportError(
949:                        f"OpenRouter {request.role!r} call exceeded the #1226 wall-clock watchdog "
950:                        f"({_ROLE_CALL_TIMEOUT_S_ENV}={_call_timeout_s}s) across the retry loop at "
951:                        f"{url} — aborting loudly so the 4-role D8 gate cannot hang (fail-closed)."
952:                    )
953:                # Step the reasoning effort DOWN per attempt (only meaningful for a reasoning role,
954:                # whose body carries a `reasoning` block). `effort is None` => reasoning DISABLED
955:                # (final resort, guarantees content): drop the `reasoning` param. Keep
956:                # `provider.require_parameters` if the body STILL carries an output-constraining
957:                # param (`response_format` / `structured_outputs`) — that pin then guarantees a
958:                # provider that HONORS those constraints (Codex diff-gate P2). Only when reasoning
959:                # was the sole constrained param do we drop require_parameters (it would otherwise
960:                # have nothing to enforce).
961:                if uses_effort_ladder and "reasoning" in body:
962:                    if effort is None:
963:                        body.pop("reasoning", None)
964:                        provider = body.get("provider")
965:                        if isinstance(provider, dict) and not (
966:                            "response_format" in body or "structured_outputs" in body
967:                        ):
968:                            provider.pop("require_parameters", None)
969:                            if not provider:
970:                                body.pop("provider", None)
971:                    else:
972:                        body["reasoning"] = {"enabled": True, "effort": effort}
973:
974:                # I-run11-008 (#1053): a transient transport failure (connection reset / WinError
975:                # 10054, read timeout, remote-protocol error) on a SINGLE role call must NOT abort the
976:                # whole 4-role run — these are intermittent OpenRouter-edge blips, not a verdict
977:                # failure. Retry the POST a bounded number of times (env-driven, LAW VI) before
978:                # fail-loud. HTTP-STATUS errors are handled separately below (not retried here).
979:                transport_retries = max(0, int(os.getenv("PG_ROLE_TRANSPORT_RETRIES", "2")))
980:                # I-beatboth-429 (#1173): rate-limit retry budget is SEPARATE from the
981:                # transport-fault budget above (a 429 is not a connection reset). Read lazily so a
982:                # monkeypatched env override is honored. The retryable-status set defaults to the
983:                # rate-limit (429) + the transient-unavailable (503) statuses.
984:                rate_limit_max = max(
985:                    0,
986:                    int(os.getenv("PG_ROLE_HTTP_RETRY_MAX", _ROLE_HTTP_RETRY_MAX_DEFAULT)),
987:                )
988:                retryable_statuses = {
989:                    int(s)
990:                    for s in os.getenv(
991:                        "PG_ROLE_HTTP_RETRY_STATUS", _ROLE_HTTP_RETRY_STATUS_DEFAULT
992:                    ).split(",")
993:                    if s.strip()
994:                }
995:                backoff_base = float(
996:                    os.getenv(
997:                        "PG_ROLE_HTTP_BACKOFF_BASE_SECONDS",
998:                        _ROLE_HTTP_BACKOFF_BASE_SECONDS_DEFAULT,
999:                    )
1000:                )
1001:                backoff_cap = float(
1002:                    os.getenv(
1003:                        "PG_ROLE_HTTP_BACKOFF_CAP_SECONDS",
1004:                        _ROLE_HTTP_BACKOFF_CAP_SECONDS_DEFAULT,
1005:                    )
1006:                )
1007:
1008:                http_response = None
1009:                rate_limit_attempt = 0
1010:                while True:
1011:                    http_response = None
1012:                    for transport_attempt in range(transport_retries + 1):
1013:                        try:
1014:                            http_response = self._http_client.post(
1015:                                url, json=body, headers=headers, timeout=_TIMEOUT_SECONDS
1016:                            )

   105:     """Score each (span ⊨ sentence) pair with the frontier LLM entailment judge; ADVISORY annotation.
   106: 
   107:     I-cap-003 (#1066): the scoring backend is now the project's LLM entailment judge
   108:     (``src.polaris_graph.llm.entailment_judge``) — a frontier OPEN-weight model (default
   109:     ``google/gemma-4-31b-it``) via OpenRouter, family-segregated from the generator, already used by
   110:     strict_verify. This REPLACES the old flan-t5/minicheck path (an old/weak F1-62.1 encoder that was
   111:     git-only and unavailable on the CPU VM → the previous ``nli_status:unavailable`` bug).
   112: 
   113:     Each pair yields a verdict ``ENTAILED | NEUTRAL | CONTRADICTED``. ``disputed`` = NEUTRAL ∪
   114:     CONTRADICTED (strict_verify's contract is "no unsupported additions"; the per-sentence ``verdict``
   115:     distinguishes them). Returns ``{nli_status:"ok", judge, model, sentences_checked, entailed_count,
   116:     neutral_count, contradicted_count, disputed_count, disputed:[…], advisory:True}``.
   117: 
   118:     FAIL-LOUD only on a genuine config error: a missing ``OPENROUTER_API_KEY`` raises
   119:     ``NliUnavailableError`` (surfaced as ``nli_status:"unavailable"``, never a silent pass). The judge is
   120:     called SYNCHRONOUSLY (NOT offloaded to a thread) so its per-call cost accumulates in the run's
   121:     ``_RUN_COST_CTX`` ContextVar and ``BudgetExceededError`` (re-raised inside ``judge.judge``) propagates
   122:     out to abort the run on a cap breach. A family-segregation error from ``_get_judge()`` PROPAGATES
   123:     (not masked as "unavailable").
   124:     """
   125:     if not pairs:                                   # fast path — needs no API key / no judge
   126:         return {
   127:             "nli_status": "ok", "judge": "llm_entailment", "sentences_checked": 0,
   128:             "sentences_scored": 0, "entailed_count": 0, "neutral_count": 0, "contradicted_count": 0,
   129:             "disputed_count": 0, "disputed": [], "judge_error_count": 0, "judge_errors": [],
   130:             "advisory": True,
   131:         }
   132: 
   133:     # Fail-loud on a genuine config error BEFORE constructing the judge (the judge __init__ raises a
   134:     # RuntimeError for a missing key that is indistinguishable from a family-collision RuntimeError —
   135:     # check the key here so only the real missing-key case maps to NliUnavailableError).
   136:     if not os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "").strip():
   137:         raise NliUnavailableError(

   144:     entailed = neutral = contradicted = judge_errors = 0
   145:     disputed: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
   146:     errors: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
   147:     for pair in pairs:
   148:         # SYNC call (no asyncio.to_thread): keeps _RUN_COST_CTX in-context so judge spend counts against
   149:         # PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN and BudgetExceededError propagates. Runs post-generation (last step), so
   150:         # blocking the loop for N sequential entailment calls is fine.

   155:         # Detect it, count it as an ERROR (NOT entailed), and surface it loudly in the annotation.

   112:

   114: - ENTAILED: every factual assertion in the SENTENCE is supported by the SPAN. Conservative paraphrase is allowed.

   116: - CONTRADICTED: the SENTENCE asserts something the SPAN explicitly disagrees with.

   974:     Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1-1: ``setdefault`` was WRONG. ``load_dotenv`` (openrouter_client import)

   976:     (e.g. ``PG_MOST_MAX_EVIDENCE=300``, ``PG_AGENTIC_WEB_PER_ROUND=6``) SURVIVED the setdefault and
   977:     silently throttled the benchmark below the slate. Fix: every numeric key is a **FLOOR** —
   978:     ``max(existing, slate)`` — so a HIGHER operator/.env value (more capability, e.g.
   979:     ``PG_LLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=600`` in .env) is KEPT, but a LOWER default is RAISED to full capability.
   980:     No silent downgrade in either direction (operator no-downgrade directive). Feature flags in
   981:     ``_BENCHMARK_FORCE_ON_FLAGS`` are FORCED ON (a benchmark feature off is a capability downgrade).
   982:     Exact-value flags in ``_BENCHMARK_FORCE_EXACT_FLAGS`` are forced to their slate value.
   983: 
   984:     Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1-2: MUST run BEFORE the sweep / live_retriever / state imports, which cache
   985:     ``PG_LIVE_CONTENT_MAX`` / ``PG_LIVE_HTTP_TIMEOUT`` / ``PG_AGENTIC_WEB_PER_ROUND`` as import-time module
   986:     constants. ``main()`` applies it before ``load_locked_questions()``; ``run_gate_b_query`` applies it
   987:     before the sweep import. ``preflight_full_capability`` then validates the LIVE module constants so a
   988:     too-late application is caught fail-closed.
   989:     """
   990:     for name, value in _FULL_CAPABILITY_BENCHMARK_SLATE.items():
   991:         if name in _BENCHMARK_FORCE_ON_FLAGS or name in _BENCHMARK_FORCE_EXACT_FLAGS:
   992:             os.environ[name] = value                       # force exact value — no silent benchmark drift
   993:             continue
   994:         try:
   995:             current = float(os.environ.get(name, value))
   996:         except (TypeError, ValueError):
   997:             current = float(value)
   998:         os.environ[name] = str(int(max(current, float(value))))   # FLOOR: raise-to-slate, keep-if-higher
   999:     # PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN is an import-time constant in openrouter_client (cached before this slate via
  1000:     # the role import chain), so the floor above only fixes the env; sync the live module global too so
  1001:     # check_run_budget enforces the floored cap and run_one_query's manifest reads it (Codex iter-1 P1-2).
  1002:     from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import set_max_cost_per_run
  1003:     set_max_cost_per_run(float(os.environ["PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN"]))
  1004: 
  1005: 
  1006: def preflight_full_capability() -> None:
  1007:     """FAIL CLOSED if the effective benchmark config is below full capability or unobservable — so a
  1008:     silent throttle (the ~40-URL bug) can NEVER reach a paid run undetected. Raises RuntimeError."""
  1009:     for name, floor in _BENCHMARK_PREFLIGHT_FLOORS.items():
  1010:         # mirror run_one_query's read of the real PG_SWEEP_* knob (defaults 12/12/40 if absent).

  1013:             eff = int(os.getenv(name, _defaults.get(name, "0")))
  1014:         except ValueError:
  1015:             raise RuntimeError(f"benchmark preflight: {name}={os.getenv(name)!r} is not an int")
  1016:         if eff < floor:
  1017:             raise RuntimeError(
  1018:                 f"benchmark preflight FAILED: {name}={eff} < full-capability floor {floor} — the run "
  1019:                 f"would be SILENTLY THROTTLED (the ~40-URL bug). Set {name}>={floor} or fix the slate."
  1020:             )
  1021:     for flag in _BENCHMARK_PREFLIGHT_REQUIRED_FLAGS:
  1022:         if os.getenv(flag, "0").strip() not in ("1", "true", "True"):
  1023:             raise RuntimeError(
  1024:                 f"benchmark preflight FAILED: {flag} is not enabled — the feature is dead-by-config "

  3328:             # concurrent children, so STORM's _RUN_COST_CTX spend can be captured NEITHER by a parent
  3329:             # copy_context read NOR by a post-hoc check_run_budget — it would silently bypass
  3330:             # PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN. Robust fix: book a CONSERVATIVE cost ENVELOPE (scaled by the STORM
  3331:             # perspective/round config) into the parent run cost and ENFORCE the cap BEFORE STORM runs.
  3332:             # STORM then runs in an ISOLATED context (its real spend is discarded from the parent total —
  3333:             # the envelope IS the parent's accounting for STORM). The envelope-inclusive isolated context
  3334:             # also makes STORM's OWN internal budget checks stop early if actual spend nears the cap.
  3335:             # Conservative by design: over-books, never under-enforces (LAW VI). The per-round estimate

    60: # section (a 0-socket provider stall — observed on the drb_72 smoke: the section
    61: # asyncio.gather hung 19 min with NO open sockets / ~0 CPU, immune to the httpx read
    62: # timeout because no socket was open to time out) gets a hard wall-clock bound: it
    63: # RETRIES once (a fresh call likely hits a healthy provider), then FAILS LOUD instead
    64: # of hanging the whole report forever. Faithfulness-neutral: only affects WHETHER a
    65: # section generates; strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / provenance are untouched.
    66: def _section_wallclock_seconds() -> int:
    67:     try:
    68:         return int(os.getenv("PG_SECTION_WALLCLOCK_SECONDS", "0"))
    69:     except ValueError:
    70:         return 0
    71: 
    72: 
    73: async def _run_section_with_wallclock(runner, plan):
    74:     """Wrap a section bounded-runner in a per-section wall-clock guard (I-arch-002
    75:     #1248). OFF (<= 0) => exact ``await runner(plan)`` (byte-identical). ON => one
    76:     wait_for, one retry, then fail-loud on a persistent wedge. ``runner`` re-runs the
    77:     full section on retry (re-acquires the semaphore + re-does the work); a cancelled
    78:     first attempt appends nothing, so there is no double-write."""
    79:     wall = _section_wallclock_seconds()
    80:     if wall <= 0:
    81:         return await runner(plan)
    82:     last_exc: BaseException | None = None
    83:     for attempt in (1, 2):
    84:         try:
    85:             return await asyncio.wait_for(runner(plan), timeout=wall)
    86:         except asyncio.TimeoutError as exc:
    87:             last_exc = exc
    88:             logger.warning(
    89:                 "[gen-wallclock] section exceeded %ds wall-clock (attempt %d/2) — "
    90:                 "likely a transient provider stall; %s",
    91:                 wall, attempt, "retrying" if attempt == 1 else "failing loud",
    92:             )
    93:     raise TimeoutError(
    94:         f"section generation exceeded {wall}s wall-clock x2 — failing loud instead "
    95:         f"of hanging the report (I-arch-002 #1248)"
    96:     ) from last_exc
    97:

   100: #

  1193:     full cap (the I-cap-005 ~40-URL silent-throttle class, one layer deeper). Returns
  1194:     ``max(PG_POST_FETCH_LOOP_BUDGET floor, fetch_cap * PG_POST_FETCH_PER_URL_BUDGET)`` — byte-identical
  1195:     for small caps (the 900s floor wins), and a conservative operator env can no longer silently win

  1197:     return max(

  1199:         max(0, int(fetch_cap)) * _env_float("PG_POST_FETCH_PER_URL_BUDGET", 4.0),
  1200:     )
  1201: 
  1202: 
  1203: def _bounded_openalex_enrich(
  1204:     url: str, title: str, stats: Optional[dict[str, int]] = None,
  1205: ) -> dict[str, Any]:
  1206:     """Wall-clock-bounded wrapper around `_openalex_enrich` (GH #554).
  1207: 
  1208:     `_openalex_enrich` issues `httpx.Client(timeout=DEFAULT_HTTP_TIMEOUT)`
  1209:     requests. `httpx`'s timeout bounds each request *phase*
  1210:     (connect/read/write/pool) but NOT total request time, so a wedged or
  1211:     byte-trickling OpenAlex response (slowloris pattern) is never
  1212:     hard-bounded. The post-`parallel_fetch` candidate loop in
  1213:     `run_live_retrieval` is synchronous, so one wedged enrich call hangs the
  1214:     whole run before it reaches any terminal verdict (#554 — demo-fatal).
  1215: 
  1216:     Run the call in a daemon thread and abandon it past
  1217:     `PG_OPENALEX_ENRICH_DEADLINE` (default 45 s = 2x the 20 s httpx phase
  1218:     timeout, covering the DOI-lookup + title-search-fallback double request,
  1219:     + margin). Enrichment is optional — the tier classifier degrades
  1220:     gracefully to title/content signals without it. `stats["enrich_timeouts"]`
  1221:     is incremented on timeout so the caller can fail-fast.
  1222:     """
  1223:     deadline = _env_float("PG_OPENALEX_ENRICH_DEADLINE", 45.0)
  1224:     holder: dict[str, Any] = {}
  1225: 
  1226:     def _worker() -> None:
  1227:         try:
  1228:             holder["value"] = _openalex_enrich(url, title)
  1229:         except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
  1230:             holder["error"] = exc
  1231: 
  1232:     worker = threading.Thread(
  1233:         target=_worker, name="openalex-enrich", daemon=True,
  1234:     )
  1235:     worker.start()
  1236:     worker.join(timeout=deadline)
  1237:     if worker.is_alive():
  1238:         if stats is not None:
  1239:             stats["enrich_timeouts"] = stats.get("enrich_timeouts", 0) + 1
  1240:         logger.warning(
  1241:             "[live_retriever] OpenAlex enrich exceeded %.0fs for %s — "
  1242:             "skipping enrichment (daemon thread abandoned)",
  1243:             deadline, url[:80],
  1244:         )
  1245:         return {}
  1246:     if "error" in holder:
  1247:         logger.debug(
  1248:             "[live_retriever] OpenAlex enrich raised for %r: %s",
  1249:             url, holder["error"],
  1250:         )

  7608:             _seam_held_reason = None
  7609:             # Manage the executor MANUALLY (NOT `with`): a `with ThreadPoolExecutor` __exit__ calls
  7610:             # shutdown(wait=True), which BLOCKS until the wedged worker finishes — defeating the
  7611:             # timeout entirely (Codex diff-gate P1). shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True) returns
  7612:             # promptly so the held manifest is written AT PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS; the

   318:     # VERDICT-level failure (fail closed for THIS claim), NOT a transport fault — so one malformed
   319:     # pass-2 response degrades a single claim to UNSUPPORTED instead of crashing the whole run.
   320:     try:
   321:         mirror_result, mirror_records = run_mirror(
   322:             recording, claim, evidence_documents, model_slug=mirror_slug
   323:         )
   324:         citation_id = _first_grounded_citation_id(mirror_records)
   325:     except (MirrorCitationError, MirrorBindingError, MirrorParseError):
   326:         mirror_failed_closed = True
   327: 
   328:     # --- stage 15 + 16: Sentinel -> Judge (only if Mirror produced a grounded claim) -----
   329:     if not mirror_failed_closed:
   330:         sentinel_result, _sentinel_records = run_sentinel(
   331:             recording, claim, evidence_documents, model_slug=sentinel_slug
   332:         )
   333:         evidence_text = "\n\n".join(doc.text for doc in evidence_documents)
   334:         # Judge fails LOUD by design: a non-enum token raises JudgeEnumError, which we do NOT
   335:         # catch — a missing/garbage arbiter verdict must propagate, never coerce to a default.
   336:         raw_judge_verdict, _judge_records = run_judge(
   337:             recording,
   338:             claim,
   339:             evidence_text,

   341:             sentinel_verdict=sentinel_result.verdict.value,
   342:             model_slug=judge_slug,
   343:         )
   344:         judge_result = raw_judge_verdict

   398: 
   399: 
   400: # Reuse the same timeout knob the self-host transport + openrouter_client use (LAW VI).
   401: # I-meta-008 FULL-POWER: the reasoning verifiers (Mirror/Judge at effort=xhigh against a 16384-token
   402: # budget) take MINUTES per claim — give them their OWN generous timeout (default 900s/15min), not the
   403: # cheap 90s shared default (which also governs retrieval/embeddings). Env-overridable (LAW VI).
   404: # NOTE: this is the PER-POST httpx timeout (bounds ONE network call). It does NOT bound the COMPOSED
   405: # retry loop (effort-ladder x transport-retries x rate-limit-backoff), which can stack to hours per
   406: # claim — that composition is bounded SEPARATELY by the #1226 wall-clock watchdog below.
   407: _TIMEOUT_SECONDS = int(os.getenv("PG_VERIFIER_LLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", "900"))
   408: 
   409: # === #1226 (I-pipe-001): blank-content bound + per-call wall-clock watchdog =====================
   410: # THE FAILURE: a reasoning verifier (e.g. GLM-5.1 Mirror under xhigh) can burn its whole token
   411: # budget on reasoning and emit EMPTY content every attempt; combined with the per-POST 900s timeout
   412: # (_TIMEOUT_SECONDS) the COMPOSED retry loop (effort-ladder/provider-failover x transport-retries x
   413: # rate-limit-backoff) can stall for HOURS per claim, so the D8 4-role gate never finishes and the
   414: # whole sweep hangs. Two RELIABILITY backstops (NEVER touch a verify/NLI/4-role verdict; on
   415: # exhaustion they re-raise the EXISTING fail-loud BlankVerdictError/RoleTransportError -> release
   416: # stays HELD; no fake/empty verdict is ever synthesized):
   417: #
   418: #   (1) PG_ROLE_BLANK_MAX_RETRIES (default 3): a HARD ceiling on the number of blank-content RETRIES.
   419: #       Implemented by TRUNCATING the per-call effort/provider ladder to `1 + max_retries` attempts.
   420: #       At the default (1 + 3 = 4) this ceiling is >= every role's existing default ladder length
   421: #       (Judge 3, Mirror/routed-Sentinel 1+PG_PROVIDER_BLANK_RETRIES=4, classifier-Sentinel 1,
   422: #       decomposition-Sentinel 3), so the truncation is the IDENTITY at default — it only bites when
   423: #       a custom PG_PROVIDER_BLANK_RETRIES / PG_FOUR_ROLE_EFFORT_LADDER pushes a ladder past the cap.
   424: #       Exhaustion fires the SAME existing `raise` at ladder-end -> the existing BlankVerdictError.
   425: #
   426: #   (2) PG_ROLE_CALL_TIMEOUT_S (default 3600s/1h): a COOPERATIVE wall-clock watchdog over the whole
   427: #       `complete()` retry loop (checked at the top of each attempt + before each rate-limit sleep).
   428: #       It does NOT interrupt an in-flight POST (httpx's _TIMEOUT_SECONDS bounds that) — it bounds the
   429: #       COMPOSITION so a stuck-in-retries call aborts LOUDLY instead of hanging the sweep. 1h is a
   430: #       deliberately-generous BACKSTOP against a wedged call, NOT a tight SLO: it sits well above any
   431: #       HEALTHY 4-role call (a healthy claim resolves in seconds-to-minutes — the ladder rarely runs
   432: #       to exhaustion, and a single blanking call returns its empty 200 quickly, it does not consume
   433: #       the full per-POST 900s). The worst-case LEGITIMATE composition (every rung waiting near the
   434: #       full _TIMEOUT_SECONDS, plus transport-retries and rate-limit backoff) CAN approach/exceed 1h;
   435: #       that path is already pathological (it IS the stall this fix targets), so aborting it loudly is
   436: #       the intended behavior, and the operator RAISES PG_ROLE_CALL_TIMEOUT_S if a slower legitimate
   437: #       budget is ever needed (LAW VI). On trip it re-raises the last BlankVerdictError (if the loop
   438: #       was blanking) else a RoleTransportError (both fail-loud; never a synthesized verdict).
   439: #
   440: # KILL-SWITCH (default-ON correct fix): PG_ROLE_BLANK_WATCHDOG (default "1"). Set "0"/"false"/"no" to
   441: # REVERT to the prior behavior — when OFF neither the ladder truncation nor the watchdog check ever
   442: # fires, so control flow is byte-identical to before #1226. (Reliability-only kill-switch pattern.)
   443: # Read LAZILY per-call (mirrors the adjacent PG_* knobs) so an override set after import is honored.
   444: _ROLE_BLANK_WATCHDOG_ENV = "PG_ROLE_BLANK_WATCHDOG"
   445: _ROLE_BLANK_MAX_RETRIES_ENV = "PG_ROLE_BLANK_MAX_RETRIES"
   446: _ROLE_BLANK_MAX_RETRIES_DEFAULT = "3"
   447: _ROLE_CALL_TIMEOUT_S_ENV = "PG_ROLE_CALL_TIMEOUT_S"
   448: _ROLE_CALL_TIMEOUT_S_DEFAULT = "3600.0"
   449: 
   450: 
   451: def role_blank_watchdog_enabled() -> bool:
   452:     """Whether the #1226 blank-bound + wall-clock watchdog is ON (kill-switch, default ON).
   453: 
   454:     `PG_ROLE_BLANK_WATCHDOG` "0"/"false"/"no" -> OFF (revert to pre-#1226 behavior, byte-identical
   455:     control flow); absent or anything else -> ON (the default-ON correct fix). LAW VI: env-driven,

  5732:                 "rewrites_proposed=%d rewrites_kept=%d rewrites_dropped_by_strict_verify=%d "
  5733:                 "redundants_dropped_by_llm_fallback=%d",
  5734:                 fact_dedup_telemetry.get("n_groups", 0),
  5735:                 fact_dedup_telemetry.get("n_redundants", 0),
  5736:                 fact_dedup_telemetry.get("n_rewrites_applied", 0),
  5737:                 rewrites_re_verified_pass,
  5738:                 rewrites_re_verified_drop,
  5739:                 fact_dedup_telemetry.get("n_drops", 0),
  5740:             )
  5741:     except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — safe-degrade per Codex review
  5742:         # I-cred-008b (#1162): the credibility-disclosure coverage gap MUST stay fail-loud.
  5743:         # The fact-dedup pass safe-degrades on its own faults, but a CredibilityPassError raised
  5744:         # by apply_disclosure_to_svs (a cited token with no credibility/origin coverage) is a
  5745:         # faithfulness abort — NEVER swallow it into a silent "continuing without dedup".
  5746:         from ..synthesis.credibility_pass import CredibilityPassError
  5747:         if isinstance(exc, CredibilityPassError):
  5748:             raise
  5749:         logger.warning(
  5750:             "[multi_section] GH#423 fact_dedup pass failed (%s); "
  5751:             "continuing without dedup", exc,
  5752:         )
  5753:         fact_dedup_telemetry = {"error": str(exc)}
  5754: 
  5755:     # I-meta-005 Phase 1 (#985, P2 note B): in on-mode (a ResearchPlan was
  5756:     # supplied) the M-44/M-47 post-generation validators route on the field-
  5757:     # invariant archetype tag carried on each SectionResult, NOT on a clinical
  5758:     # title literal. OFF-mode keeps title-keyed routing byte-identically.
  5759:     _use_archetype = research_plan is not None
  5760: 
  5761:     # M-44 (2026-04-22): post-generation same-sentence validator +
  5762:     # one-shot regeneration. For each primary-eligible section, scan
  5763:     # verified prose for named-trial tokens; each trial mention must
  5764:     # cite a matching M-42e primary ev_id in the same sentence or
  5765:     # immediately adjacent (prev/next) sentence. Violations trigger
  5766:     # ONE regeneration with explicit primary_cite_required ev_id list
  5767:     # appended to the section's focus prompt. If still missing after
  5768:     # regen, emit `m44_primary_citation_incomplete` telemetry and
  5769:     # keep the original verified text (honest ship).
  5770:     m44_validator_violations: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
  5771:     if m44_primary_by_anchor:
  5772:         # First validator pass — M-44 pass-3 (Codex audit): record the
  5773:         # per-section violation count here so the regen replacement
  5774:         # criterion can compare against it. Pre-pass-3 the comparison
  5775:         # was against an empty list (dead code path — regens were
  5776:         # always rejected even when they had fewer violations).
  5777:         sections_needing_regen: list[int] = []
  5778:         first_pass_violations_by_idx: dict[int, int] = {}
  5779:         for idx, sr in enumerate(section_results):
  5780:             if sr.dropped_due_to_failure or not sr.verified_text:
  5781:                 continue
  5782:             if not _section_is_primary_eligible(
  5783:                 title=sr.title, archetype=sr.archetype,
  5784:                 use_archetype=_use_archetype,
  5785:             ):
  5786:                 continue
  5787:             viols = _m44_validate_primary_same_sentence(
  5788:                 sr.verified_text,
  5789:                 m44_primary_by_anchor,
  5790:                 sr.biblio_slice,
  5791:             )
  5792:             if viols:
  5793:                 sections_needing_regen.append(idx)
  5794:                 first_pass_violations_by_idx[idx] = len(viols)
  5795: 
  5796:         # Regen pass (Codex audit finding #1): one attempt per section
  5797:         # with a focus-level hint that enumerates the required primary
  5798:         # ev_ids. Only sections matching the violation were marked.
  5799:         if sections_needing_regen:
  5800:             logger.info(
  5801:                 "[multi_section] M-44 validator regen pass for %d "
  5802:                 "section(s)", len(sections_needing_regen),
  5803:             )
  5804:             regen_plans_by_idx: dict[int, SectionPlan] = {}
  5805:             for idx in sections_needing_regen:
  5806:                 sr = section_results[idx]
  5807:                 # Build an augmented focus containing the required ev_ids.
  5808:                 required_ev_ids: list[str] = []
  5809:                 for anchor, evs in m44_primary_by_anchor.items():
  5810:                     if not evs:
  5811:                         continue
  5812:                     # Only list primaries assigned to this section's
  5813:                     # subset (in ev_ids_assigned), so the hint is
  5814:                     # actionable.
  5815:                     for ev in evs:
  5816:                         if ev in sr.ev_ids_assigned:
  5817:                             required_ev_ids.append(ev)
  5818:                             break
  5819:                 if not required_ev_ids:
  5820:                     continue
  5821:                 # Match plans by title; SectionPlan.title is unique.
  5822:                 orig_plan = next(
  5823:                     (p for p in plans if p.title == sr.title), None,
  5824:                 )
  5825:                 if orig_plan is None:
  5826:                     continue
  5827:                 hint = (
  5828:                     f"\n\nREQUIRED: When you name any of the following "
  5829:                     f"trials by short-name, cite the corresponding "
  5830:                     f"primary-publication evidence ID in the same "
  5831:                     f"sentence or the immediately adjacent sentence: "
  5832:                     f"{', '.join(required_ev_ids)}."
  5833:                 )
  5834:                 regen_plans_by_idx[idx] = SectionPlan(
  5835:                     title=orig_plan.title,
  5836:                     focus=orig_plan.focus + hint,
  5837:                     ev_ids=orig_plan.ev_ids,
  5838:                     # I-meta-005 Phase 1 (#985, P1-13): preserve archetype.
  5839:                     archetype=getattr(orig_plan, "archetype", ""),
  5840:                 )
  5841:             # Run regens in parallel with the same semaphore.
  5842:             regen_items = list(regen_plans_by_idx.items())
  5843:             regen_tasks = [
  5844:                 _run_section_with_wallclock(_bounded_run, plan) for _, plan in regen_items
  5845:             ]
  5846:             regen_results = await asyncio.gather(
  5847:                 *regen_tasks, return_exceptions=True,
  5848:             )
  5849:             for (idx, plan), regen_result in zip(regen_items, regen_results):
  5850:                 if isinstance(regen_result, Exception):
  5851:                     # I-cred-008b (#1162): a credibility-disclosure coverage gap raised during M-44
  5852:                     # regen MUST stay fail-loud — never swallowed into "continue without the regen".
  5853:                     # return_exceptions=True captured it as a value; re-raise it here.
  5854:                     from ..synthesis.credibility_pass import CredibilityPassError

  5856:                         raise regen_result

  5858:                         "[multi_section] M-44 regen raised for %s: %s",
  5859:                         plan.title, regen_result,

   635: 
   636: def plan_research(
   637:     question: str,
   638:     *,
   639:     planner_llm: Callable[[str], str],
   640:     max_subqueries: int = DEFAULT_MAX_SUBQUERIES,
   641:     min_subqueries: int = MIN_SUBQUERIES,
   642: ) -> ResearchPlan:
   643:     """Produce a `ResearchPlan` from `question` using ONE Writer call (plus at
   644:     most one bounded lower-bound retry).
   645: 
   646:     Args:
   647:         question: The raw research question (stored verbatim on the plan).
   648:         planner_llm: Injected Writer callable `prompt -> response_text`. This
   649:             is the ONLY way the planner reaches an LLM; the module never
   650:             constructs an `OpenRouterClient` or a live HTTP client. Tests pass
   651:             a fake; production passes the real Writer.
   652:         max_subqueries: UPPER bound; >this is merged/truncated deterministically.
   653:         min_subqueries: LOWER bound that triggers ONE fail-loud retry.
   654: 
   655:     Returns:
   656:         A validated `ResearchPlan` (frame + sub_queries + archetype outline).
   657: 
   658:     Raises:
   659:         ValueError: empty question.
   660:         PlannerError: malformed / unusable planner output (LAW II — no silent
   661:             fallback to the clause-splitter).
   662:     """
   663:     if not question or not question.strip():
   664:         raise ValueError("question must be non-empty.")
   665:     if not callable(planner_llm):
   666:         raise TypeError("planner_llm must be a callable[[str], str].")
   667: 
   668:     prompt = _build_prompt(
   669:         question, more_facets=False, min_subqueries=min_subqueries,
   670:     )
   671:     raw = planner_llm(prompt)
   672:     plan = _parse_plan(raw, question.strip())
   673:     plan.sub_queries = _merge_truncate_subqueries(
   674:         plan.sub_queries, max_subqueries=max_subqueries,
   675:     )
   676: 
   677:     # LOWER-bound policy (brief §2.1): a fail-loud retry, NOT padding. If the
   678:     # honest count is short, ask once for more facets. If still short, accept
   679:     # the honest smaller count for a genuinely narrow question and log.
   680:     if len(plan.sub_queries) < min_subqueries:
   681:         logger.info(
   682:             "[research_planner] sub_query count %d < min %d — retrying once "
   683:             "for more facets",
   684:             len(plan.sub_queries), min_subqueries,
   685:         )
   686:         retry_prompt = _build_prompt(
   687:             question, more_facets=True, min_subqueries=min_subqueries,
   688:         )
   689:         retry_raw = planner_llm(retry_prompt)
   690:         retry_plan = _parse_plan(retry_raw, question.strip())
   691:         retry_plan.sub_queries = _merge_truncate_subqueries(
   692:             retry_plan.sub_queries, max_subqueries=max_subqueries,
   693:         )
   694:         # Keep whichever response carried more honest facets.
   695:         if len(retry_plan.sub_queries) > len(plan.sub_queries):
   696:             plan = retry_plan
   697:         if len(plan.sub_queries) < min_subqueries:
   698:             logger.info(
   699:                 "[research_planner] accepting honest narrow count %d "
   700:                 "(< min %d) after retry — NOT padding",
   701:                 len(plan.sub_queries), min_subqueries,
   702:             )
   703:     # I-meta-005 Phase 3 (#987): FAIL-CLOSED post-finalization facet validation.
   704:     # The sub_queries list is now FINAL (post-truncation / retry-winner). Any

 succeeded in 918ms:

     2:

     5: byte-identical to the legacy clause-split + clinical-PICO + `_ALLOWED_SECTIONS`

     7: 
     8: DESIGN (brief §2.1):
     9: - `ResearchFrame` — a generalized PICO that carries NO clinical fields:
    10:   entities / relations / metrics / comparators / constraints + a `claim_type`
    11:   from a field-invariant enum. A housing, physics, or trade-policy question
    12:   produces a usable frame; nothing is clinical-specific.
    13: - `plan_research(question, *, planner_llm)` makes ONE normal Writer call (plus
    14:   AT MOST ONE bounded retry when the honest sub-query count is short). The
    15:   Writer is an INJECTED callable `Callable[[str], str]`, so this module never
    16:   constructs an `OpenRouterClient` or a live HTTP client — build + smoke are
    17:   spend-free. Production threads the existing Writer through the callable.
    18: - Strict JSON parse. Malformed -> raise `PlannerError` (LAW II). There is NO
    19:   silent fallback to the clause-splitter; the dual path lives at the caller.
    20: - Sub-query count is HONEST (brief §2.1):
    21:   * UPPER bound `DEFAULT_MAX_SUBQUERIES` (40): merge/truncate deterministically.
    22:   * LOWER bound is a FAIL-LOUD retry, not deterministic padding: when fewer
    23:     than `MIN_SUBQUERIES` facets come back, retry ONCE asking for more. If a
    24:     genuinely narrow question still yields fewer, ACCEPT the honest smaller
    25:     count and log — never fabricate facets to hit a target.
    26: - `ResearchFrame.to_anchor_protocol()` exposes the frame's own tokens as an
    27:   anchor-protocol dict so planner sub-queries validate against the frame
    28:   (brief §2.4, validator adapter).
    29: - `serialize_plan_canonical()` emits canonical JSON (sort_keys, fixed
    30:   separators) so the caller can SHA-pin the `ResearchPlan` BEFORE retrieval
    31:   (gap #19 extension, brief §2.1).
    32: 
    33: The archetype tag vocabulary is owned by the generator
    34: (`multi_section_generator._SECTION_ARCHETYPES`); the planner imports it so the
    35: two halves of the dual path share ONE source of truth and the planner stays

   704:     # The sub_queries list is now FINAL (post-truncation / retry-winner). Any
   705:     # outline section whose facet mapping is empty / stale / out-of-range, OR a
   706:     # planned sub_query mapped to NO section, makes the plan-sufficiency contract
   707:     # vacuous — so refuse the plan BEFORE any retrieval/generation spend.
   708:     _validate_outline_facet_mapping(plan)
   709:     return plan

   711: 
   712: def _validate_outline_facet_mapping(plan: ResearchPlan) -> None:
   713:     """FAIL-CLOSED on-mode facet-mapping validation (brief §2.1b / §2.3a),
   714:     run AFTER `plan.sub_queries` is FINAL. Every section MUST:
   715:       * declare ≥1 `sub_query_index`,
   716:       * have every index in range of the FINAL `sub_queries`, AND
   717:       * carry `evidence_target ≥ 1`,
   718:     and the UNION of all sections' `sub_query_indices` MUST equal
   719:     `set(range(len(sub_queries)))` (no orphaned planned facet escapes the gate).
   720:     Any violation raises `MalformedPlanError` (zero spend). Pure / no-network.
   721:     """
   722:     n_sub = len(plan.sub_queries)
   723:     covered: set[int] = set()
   724:     for section in plan.outline:
   725:         indices = list(section.sub_query_indices)
   726:         if not indices:
   727:             raise MalformedPlanError(
   728:                 f"outline section {section.title!r} has no sub_query_indices "
   729:                 "(every on-mode section must map ≥1 planned sub-query)"
   730:             )
   731:         if int(section.evidence_target) < 1:
   732:             raise MalformedPlanError(
   733:                 f"outline section {section.title!r} has evidence_target="
   734:                 f"{section.evidence_target} (on-mode requires ≥1)"
   735:             )
   736:         for idx in indices:
   737:             if idx < 0 or idx >= n_sub:
   738:                 raise MalformedPlanError(
   739:                     f"outline section {section.title!r} maps sub_query_index "
   740:                     f"{idx} out of range for {n_sub} final sub_queries"
   741:                 )
   742:             covered.add(idx)
   743:     expected = set(range(n_sub))
   744:     if covered != expected:
   745:         orphaned = sorted(expected - covered)
   746:         raise MalformedPlanError(
   747:             f"planned sub_queries {orphaned} are mapped to no outline section "
   748:             "(every planned facet must be covered by some section)"
   749:         )

  1385: def load_locked_questions(slugs: tuple[str, ...] | None = None) -> list[dict]:
  1386:     """Resolve the locked benchmark question(s) by FILTERING the existing `SWEEP_QUERIES`.
  1387: 
  1388:     `SWEEP_QUERIES` (in `scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3`) is the single runtime source of
  1389:     truth the sweep AND the routing tests already key off; this loader is a thin filter
  1390:     over it, NOT a new question source (§0 of the I-meta-007e brief). Imported LAZILY so
  1391:     importing `run_gate_b` opens no socket and pulls no big sweep file at module load.
  1392: 
  1393:     `slugs=None` resolves all 5 locked slugs (in `LOCKED_BENCHMARK_SLUGS` order); a tuple
  1394:     resolves that subset. Each requested slug must be one of the 5 locked slugs AND present
  1395:     in `SWEEP_QUERIES` exactly once with a `domain` — otherwise this fails LOUD (LAW II),
  1396:     never a silent skip / empty run.
  1397: 
  1398:     CALLER CONTRACT (env-preservation): importing `scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3` triggers
  1399:     `load_dotenv(override=False)` at its module top, which mutates `os.environ` from `.env`.
  1400:     The `--list` path (enumeration-only, NO spend) wraps the CALL to this loader in an
  1401:     os.environ snapshot/restore so it leaves the process env byte-identical; the real-run
  1402:     path (`--only`/`--all`) lets `.env` stand because the live run needs the keys.
  1403:     """
  1404:     requested = LOCKED_BENCHMARK_SLUGS if slugs is None else tuple(slugs)
  1405:     unknown = [s for s in requested if s not in LOCKED_BENCHMARK_SLUGS]
  1406:     if unknown:
  1407:         raise ValueError(
  1408:             f"load_locked_questions: slug(s) {unknown!r} are not locked benchmark slugs. "
  1409:             f"Valid locked slugs: {list(LOCKED_BENCHMARK_SLUGS)}."
  1410:         )
  1411: 
  1412:     from scripts.run_honest_sweep_r3 import SWEEP_QUERIES
  1413: 
  1414:     resolved: list[dict] = []
  1415:     for slug in requested:
  1416:         matches = [q for q in SWEEP_QUERIES if q.get("slug") == slug]
  1417:         if not matches:
  1418:             raise ValueError(
  1419:                 f"load_locked_questions: locked slug {slug!r} is NOT registered in "
  1420:                 f"SWEEP_QUERIES — the loader filters the existing registration (no hardcoded "
  1421:                 f"slug->question fallback). Fix the SWEEP_QUERIES registration."
  1422:             )
  1423:         if len(matches) > 1:
  1424:             raise ValueError(
  1425:                 f"load_locked_questions: locked slug {slug!r} is registered {len(matches)} "
  1426:                 f"times in SWEEP_QUERIES; a benchmark slug must route to exactly one domain "
  1427:                 f"(fail-closed)."
  1428:             )
  1429:         entry = matches[0]
  1430:         if not entry.get("domain"):
  1431:             raise ValueError(
  1432:                 f"load_locked_questions: SWEEP_QUERIES entry for slug {slug!r} has no domain; "
  1433:                 f"the 4-role builder keys the frozen contract by (domain template, slug) — a "
  1434:                 f"domain-less registration cannot route (fail-closed)."
  1435:             )
  1436:         resolved.append(entry)
  1437:     return resolved
  1438: 
  1439: 
  1440: def _format_list_preview(questions: list[dict]) -> str:
  1441:     """Build the `--list` / `--dry-run` preview text — NO spend, NO network, NO env mutation.
  1442: 
  1443:     Uses ONLY the PURE readers (`four_role_transport_mode`, `verifier_model_slugs`,
  1444:     `assert_four_role_families_distinct`) — none opens a socket or mutates process env; each
  1445:     reads only the in-memory architecture lock / process env. It calls NONE of
  1446:     `run_gate_b_query` / `enable_four_role_mode` / `preflight_four_role_transport` /
  1447:     `build_gate_b_transport`. Surfaces, for the blind operator, exactly what a real run WOULD
  1448:     do before any money is authorized: the resolved questions, the active transport mode, the
  1449:     4 role slugs+families, and a DESCRIPTIVE preflight plan (not an executed preflight).
  1450:     """
  1451:     mode = four_role_transport_mode()
  1452:     families = assert_four_role_families_distinct()  # in-memory lock read; asserts 4 distinct
  1453:     verifier_slugs = verifier_model_slugs()
  1454:     generator_family = families.get("generator", "<unknown>")
  1455: 
  1456:     lines: list[str] = []
  1457:     lines.append("=" * 72)
  1458:     lines.append("GATE-B 4-ROLE BENCHMARK -- NO-SPEND PREVIEW (--list / --dry-run)")

   118: 
   119: # Probe token magnitudes (LAW VI, env-overridable). A liveness probe only needs to prove the route
   120: # RESOLVES + the production call shape is ACCEPTED — NOT to emit a real verdict — so the per-role
   121: # reasoning/output budgets the production body-builder sets (Mirror 24k / Judge 16k / decomp-Sentinel
   122: # 16k) are clamped DOWN to a cheap, but STILL-VALID, magnitude. The clamp PRESERVES the production
   123: # invariant that top-level max_tokens must exceed the reasoning budget (openrouter_role_transport
   124: # lines 526-533); shrinking only the magnitudes keeps the provider/reasoning routing — the very
   125: # `require_parameters`-filtered keys whose ABSENCE is the P1-2 false-pass class — byte-identical to
   126: # production. Cost is driven by tokens actually GENERATED (a 1-token "ok" reply stops at EOS), not the
   127: # ceiling, so this is ~free while remaining a faithful, routable body.
   128: _PROBE_REASONING_TOKENS = int(os.getenv("PG_PREFLIGHT_PROBE_REASONING_TOKENS", "16"))
   129: _PROBE_MAX_TOKENS = int(os.getenv("PG_PREFLIGHT_PROBE_MAX_TOKENS", "32"))
   130: 
   131: 
   132: def _clamp_probe_budget(body: dict) -> dict:
   133:     """Shrink the production body's token MAGNITUDES to a cheap probe size WITHOUT changing its shape.
   134: 
   135:     Mutates ``body`` in place and returns it. Preserves every routing key the production builder set
   136:     (``provider`` / ``reasoning`` enabled+effort or numeric cap) — those are exactly what OpenRouter's
   137:     ``require_parameters`` filters on, so dropping them would re-introduce the P1-2 false-pass. Only the
   138:     numeric budgets shrink, and they stay COHERENT with the production rule "top-level max_tokens must
   139:     exceed the reasoning budget":
   140:       - numeric reasoning cap (Mirror ``reasoning.max_tokens``): cap -> _PROBE_REASONING_TOKENS, and
   141:         top-level max_tokens -> a value STRICTLY above it (cap + margin).
   142:       - effort reasoning (Judge / decomposition-Sentinel ``reasoning.effort``): effort is KEPT
   143:         (mutually exclusive with reasoning.max_tokens, NOT with top-level max_tokens), only the
   144:         top-level max_tokens is lowered to the small probe ceiling.
   145:       - no reasoning block (classifier Sentinel): just lower the top-level max_tokens.
   146:     """
   147:     reasoning = body.get("reasoning")
   148:     if isinstance(reasoning, dict) and "max_tokens" in reasoning:
   149:         # Mirror's NUMERIC reasoning cap path: shrink the cap, keep top-level strictly above it.
   150:         reasoning["max_tokens"] = _PROBE_REASONING_TOKENS
   151:         body["max_tokens"] = max(_PROBE_MAX_TOKENS, _PROBE_REASONING_TOKENS + 8)
   152:     else:
   153:         # Effort-based reasoning (Judge / decomp-Sentinel) OR no reasoning (classifier Sentinel):
   154:         # effort and top-level max_tokens are NOT mutually exclusive, so keep the effort block as-is
   155:         # and only lower the top-level ceiling. (Never set max_tokens=1 — a provider may reject it.)
   156:         body["max_tokens"] = _PROBE_MAX_TOKENS
   157:     return body
   158: 
   159: 
   160: def _build_probe_request(role: str, slug: str):
   161:     """Build the production verifier request body+endpoint+headers for ``role`` in the ACTIVE transport
   162:     mode, REUSING the production transports' OWN request-construction helpers (NOT a re-derived generic
   163:     body). Returns ``(endpoint, headers, body)``.
   164: 
   165:     - ``self_host``: ``role_endpoint(role)`` (per-role PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL + PG_<ROLE>_API_KEY, NEVER the
   166:       OpenRouter key) + the ``/v1/chat/completions`` path the self-host transport appends + ``_build_body``
   167:       (the passthrough allowlist + the decomposition-Sentinel max_tokens floor). Authorization is OMITTED
   168:       when PG_<ROLE>_API_KEY is unset — EXACTLY as ``OpenAICompatibleRoleTransport.complete`` does (a
   169:       keyless vLLM is valid; sending an empty ``Bearer `` is a false-FAIL, the P1-1 class).
   170:     - ``openrouter``: ``openrouter_role_endpoint(role)`` (OpenRouter base + key + benchmark slug) + the
   171:       ``/chat/completions`` path + ``_build_openrouter_body`` (the per-role reasoning + provider routing
   172:       the production verifier call carries — the P1-2 class). Headers mirror the production transport.
   173: 
   174:     The token budgets are then clamped to a cheap probe size via ``_clamp_probe_budget`` (route resolves,
   175:     call shape accepted, ~free). Slug RESOLUTION stays production-exact (the caller passes the
   176:     ``verifier_model_slugs()`` slug, which == the builder's body["model"] on both routes)."""
   177:     from src.polaris_graph.roles.role_transport import RoleRequest
   178: 
   179:     # A minimal 1-token-ish completion request — prompt-only so _normalize_messages builds a clean
   180:     # single user turn (it raises without prompt/messages). Empty params: the role-specific helpers
   181:     # fill in reasoning/provider/max_tokens per role.
   182:     request = RoleRequest(role=role, model_slug=slug, prompt="ok", params={})
   183: 
   184:     from scripts.dr_benchmark.run_gate_b import four_role_transport_mode
   185: 
   186:     mode = four_role_transport_mode()
   187:     if mode == "self_host":
   188:         from src.polaris_graph.roles.openai_compatible_transport import (
   189:             _CHAT_COMPLETIONS_PATH,
   190:             _build_body,
   191:             _normalize_messages,
   192:             role_endpoint,
   193:         )
   194: 
   195:         base_url, api_key, model_slug = role_endpoint(role)
   196:         normalized_messages = _normalize_messages(request)
   197:         body = _clamp_probe_budget(_build_body(request, model_slug, normalized_messages))
   198:         endpoint = f"{base_url}{_CHAT_COMPLETIONS_PATH}"
   199:         # No-leak / keyless-vLLM: Authorization ONLY when a per-role key is set (mirrors
   200:         # OpenAICompatibleRoleTransport.complete lines 482-484) — never an empty `Bearer `.
   201:         headers = {"Content-Type": "application/json"}
   202:         if api_key:
   203:             headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {api_key}"
   204:         return endpoint, headers, body
   205: 
   206:     # openrouter (benchmark, default)
   207:     from src.polaris_graph.roles.openai_compatible_transport import _normalize_messages
   208:     from src.polaris_graph.roles.openrouter_role_transport import (

   216:     body = _clamp_probe_budget(_build_openrouter_body(request, model_slug, normalized_messages))
   217:     endpoint = f"{base_url}{_CHAT_COMPLETIONS_PATH}"
